**[12. DEVELOPER ACKNOWLEDGMENTS AND LENDER REQUIREMENTS REGARDING DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

The Development Agreement between Borrower and the City of Lakemont is a critical component of the Project\'s legal and financial framework. This section sets forth Lender\'s specific requirements and concerns regarding the Development Agreement. Borrower and its counsel (Thornfield & Pratt LLP, Attn: Catherine M. Albrecht) should carefully consider each requirement below in negotiating and drafting the Development Agreement.

**Lender Review Right.** The Development Agreement shall be delivered to Lender in draft form not less than fifteen (15) business days prior to its proposed execution date. The Development Agreement shall not be executed until Lender has confirmed in writing that it is in form and substance acceptable to Lender. Lender\'s review shall not be unreasonably delayed.

**Required Development Agreement Provisions (from Lender\'s Perspective):**

> \(a\) **No Reversionary Interest / Defeasible Fee.** The Development Agreement shall not grant the City of Lakemont a reversionary interest, right of re-entry, possibility of reverter, or any other interest that would render Borrower\'s fee simple title defeasible. Lender acknowledges that City Council Resolution R-2025-017 (adopted February 18, 2025), at condition #8, contemplates a \"reversionary interest\" as a clawback mechanism in the event Phase I is not Substantially Complete within 36 months of land conveyance. Borrower must negotiate an alternative mechanism that satisfies the City\'s policy objective of ensuring timely Phase I completion without impairing Borrower\'s fee simple title or Lender\'s security interest. Acceptable alternatives are described below.
>
> \(b\) **Lender Cure Rights.** The Development Agreement shall include provisions granting Lender the right (but not the obligation) to receive prompt written notice of any Borrower default under the Development Agreement and a reasonable opportunity to cure such default. Lender\'s additional cure period shall be not less than thirty (30) days beyond Borrower\'s cure period for monetary defaults and not less than sixty (60) days beyond Borrower\'s cure period for non-monetary defaults, with such periods extended as necessary if Lender is diligently pursuing a cure that requires additional time (including time necessary to obtain possession of the Project site through foreclosure or appointment of a receiver).
>
> \(c\) **Lender Consent to Amendments.** No material amendment, modification, supplement, or termination of the Development Agreement shall be effective without Lender\'s prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed.
>
> \(d\) **Subordination of City Interests.** Any lien, encumbrance, or security interest that the City may hold or acquire in the Project site (other than the City\'s reversionary or re-entry rights, which must be eliminated per subsection (a) above) shall be subordinated to Lender\'s mortgage pursuant to a subordination agreement in form and substance acceptable to Lender.
>
> \(e\) **Foreclosure / Deed-in-Lieu.** The Development Agreement shall include a provision permitting assignment of the Development Agreement to a Lender-designee or to a third-party purchaser at a foreclosure sale (or pursuant to a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure) without the City\'s prior consent, or if City consent is required, such consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed, and the City shall respond within thirty (30) days of request.
>
> \(f\) **Estoppel Certificates.** The Development Agreement shall require the City to deliver estoppel certificates to Lender upon reasonable request (not more than twice per calendar year), certifying the status of the Development Agreement, the existence or absence of any outstanding defaults, and such other customary matters as Lender may reasonably request.

**Specific Concern --- Resolution R-2025-017, Condition #8.**

Lender has reviewed City Council Resolution R-2025-017 and notes that Condition #8 requires Borrower to grant the City a \"reversionary interest\" in the Project site if Phase I is not Substantially Complete within 36 months of land conveyance. Lender cannot commit to a loan secured by a mortgage on property that is subject to a reversionary interest or any form of defeasible fee estate. A reversionary interest in favor of the City would constitute a superior possessory right that would: (i) be uninsurable under standard ALTA title insurance policies; (ii) effectively subordinate Lender\'s first-priority mortgage to the City\'s right to reclaim the property upon the occurrence of the stated condition; (iii) prevent Lender from exercising standard foreclosure remedies; and (iv) render the entire Construction Facility unsecurable.

This condition, if incorporated into the Development Agreement as stated in the Resolution, would make the Loan impossible to close.

Borrower must resolve this issue with the City prior to Loan closing. Lender suggests the following alternatives for Borrower to propose to the City as substitute clawback mechanisms that would satisfy the City\'s legitimate interest in ensuring timely completion while preserving Borrower\'s fee simple absolute title and Lender\'s first-priority security interest:

> \(i\) **Contractual Repurchase Option.** A contractual option in favor of the City to repurchase the Project site, exercisable only upon (A) Borrower\'s failure to achieve Substantial Completion of Phase I within the agreed timeframe, (B) expiration of all cure periods (including Lender\'s extended cure period), and (C) Lender\'s prior written consent or full satisfaction of the outstanding Loan balance. The repurchase price would be the lesser of the original purchase price paid by Borrower or the then-current fair market value, as determined by an independent MAI appraiser.
>
> \(ii\) **Performance Escrow / Additional Letter of Credit.** An additional escrow deposit or irrevocable standby letter of credit held by a mutually acceptable escrow agent, to be drawn upon by the City in the event of a completion failure. This mechanism converts the City\'s remedy from a real-property interest to a contractual/financial claim and preserves the integrity of Borrower\'s fee simple title.
>
> \(iii\) **Liquidated Damages.** A provision for liquidated damages payable to the City in a predetermined amount upon Borrower\'s failure to achieve timely completion, representing a reasonable pre-estimate of the City\'s actual damages. Such liquidated damages would constitute an unsecured contractual obligation of Borrower, not a lien on the property.
>
> \(iv\) **Enhanced Completion Guaranty.** An enhanced personal and/or trust guaranty from the Guarantors, guaranteeing completion of Phase I within the agreed timeframe and providing the City with a direct contractual remedy against the Guarantors (and their assets, other than the Project site) in the event of a completion failure.
>
> \(v\) **Right of First Refusal.** A right of first refusal in favor of the City with respect to any disposition of the Project site, subordinate to Lender\'s mortgage and exercisable only upon a default-related disposition (other than a foreclosure or deed-in-lieu).

In all cases, Lender\'s mortgage must remain a first-priority lien on the Project site and all improvements, and Borrower\'s fee simple title must remain absolute and indefeasible. Lender is prepared to work cooperatively with Borrower and its counsel to identify a structure that is acceptable to both Lender and the City, and Lender encourages Borrower to initiate discussions with the City on this issue at the earliest practicable date.

**[13. MISCELLANEOUS TERMS]{.underline}**

**Governing Law:** The Loan and all Loan Documents shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, without regard to conflict of laws principles.

**Venue:** Any litigation arising out of the Loan or the Loan Documents shall be brought in the state or federal courts located in Cook County, Illinois.

**Notices:** All notices shall be in writing and delivered to the following addresses (or such other addresses as may be designated in writing):

> *To Borrower:* Granite City Development LLC 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200 Chicago, IL 60607
>
> Attention: Marcus J. Pellegrini, Manager/CEO
>
> *With copy to:* Thornfield & Pratt LLP 311 South Wacker Drive, Suite 5400 Chicago, IL 60606
>
> Attention: Catherine M. Albrecht
>
> *To Lender:* First Prairie National Bank 135 South LaSalle Street Chicago, IL 60603
>
> Attention: Sandra L. Chen, Senior Vice President
>
> Real Estate Lending Division

**Expenses:** Borrower shall be responsible for all costs and expenses incurred by Lender in connection with the Loan, including Lender\'s counsel fees, title insurance premiums, appraisal fees, environmental review and consulting costs, construction monitoring fees, survey costs, and recording charges, whether or not the Loan closes.

**Confidentiality:** The terms of this Term Sheet and the Loan shall be treated as confidential by both parties, subject to standard exceptions for disclosure required by law, regulation, legal process, bank examination, or in connection with potential assignments or participations (subject to customary confidentiality agreements).

**Relationship Manager:** Sandra L. Chen, Senior Vice President, Real Estate Lending Division, First Prairie National Bank.

**[14. NON-BINDING NATURE AND EXPIRATION]{.underline}**

**Non-Binding Disclaimer.** This Preliminary Term Sheet is intended as a summary of the principal terms on which First Prairie National Bank may consider providing the Construction Facility described herein. This Term Sheet is for discussion purposes only and does not constitute a commitment or obligation to lend. Any commitment to lend shall be subject to: (a) satisfactory completion of all due diligence, including financial, legal, environmental, title, and construction review; (b) approval by Lender\'s Credit Committee in its sole discretion; (c) execution and delivery of mutually acceptable definitive loan documentation prepared by Lender\'s counsel; (d) satisfaction of all conditions precedent set forth herein and in the definitive loan documentation; and (e) satisfaction of all applicable regulatory and legal requirements, including any requirements of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency.

**Expiration.** This Term Sheet shall expire and be of no further force or effect unless accepted by Borrower in the manner described below on or before May \_\_, 2025.

**Acceptance.** Acceptance shall be by counter-signature of Borrower below and delivery of the executed Term Sheet to Lender, together with a good-faith deposit of \$250,000 (the \"Good-Faith Deposit\"). The Good-Faith Deposit shall be refundable if Lender, in its sole discretion, declines to proceed to definitive documentation after completion of its due diligence review. If the Loan closes, the Good-Faith Deposit shall be applied toward the commitment fee described in Section 2.2. If Borrower withdraws its application or fails to satisfy conditions precedent within the required timeframes, the Good-Faith Deposit shall be retained by Lender as compensation for due diligence costs incurred.

**FOR DISCUSSION PURPOSES ONLY --- NON-BINDING**

**\[SIGNATURE PAGE FOLLOWS\]**

**SIGNATURE PAGE** **PRELIMINARY TERM SHEET --- CONSTRUCTION LOAN FACILITY** **LAKEMONT STATION COMMONS**

**FIRST PRAIRIE NATIONAL BANK**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Sandra L. Chen

Title: Senior Vice President, Real Estate Lending Division

Date: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

**ACKNOWLEDGED AND ACCEPTED:**

**GRANITE CITY DEVELOPMENT LLC**

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Marcus J. Pellegrini

Title: Manager/CEO

Date: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

**[APPENDIX --- SCHEDULES REFERENCE]{.underline}**

The following schedules will be prepared and negotiated as part of the definitive loan documentation. They are not attached to this Preliminary Term Sheet but are referenced herein for completeness:

**Schedule A:** Project Description and Phasing Plan (detailed descriptions of Phases I through IV, including square footages, unit counts, timelines, and milestone dates)

**Schedule B:** Approved Construction Budget (detailed line-item budget for Phase I and Phase II, including hard costs, soft costs, contingencies, and interest reserve)

**Schedule C:** Sources and Uses of Funds

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Source**                                                **Amount**
  --------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Borrower Equity (Lakeshore Capital + Pellegrini Trust)    \$85,000,000

  Construction Loan (First Prairie National Bank)           \$210,000,000

  TIF Reimbursement (Lakemont Central TIF District)         \$48,000,000

  Land Discount (City conveyance at 50% appraised value)    \$9,200,000

  Permit/Fee Waivers                                        \$1,400,000

  Additional Debt / Mezzanine Financing (Phases III & IV)   \$58,400,000

  **Total Project Cost**                                    **\$412,000,000**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Schedule D:** Insurance Requirements (detailed specifications for all required coverage)

**Schedule E:** Permitted Exceptions to Title (as defined in Section 4.2)

**Schedule F:** Form of Compliance Certificate (quarterly and annual certifications to be delivered by Borrower)

**Schedule G:** Form of Draw Request (including required supporting documentation and certifications)

**END OF PRELIMINARY TERM SHEET**

Dear Mr. Pellegrini:

This side letter (this \"**Side Letter**\") sets forth certain additional agreements between Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP, a Delaware limited partnership acting through its general partner, Lakeshore Capital Management LLC (\"**Investor**\" or \"**Lakeshore Capital**\"), and Granite City Development LLC, an Illinois limited liability company (\"**Developer**\"), regarding the investment by Investor in Developer in connection with the Lakemont Station Commons project, as more fully described herein. result:
**LAKESHORE CAPITAL PARTNERS FUND III LP** 200 East Randolph Street, Suite 3100 Chicago, Illinois 60601

February 10, 2025

Granite City Development LLC 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200 Chicago, Illinois 60607

Attention: Marcus J. Pellegrini, Manager/CEO

**Re: Side Letter Regarding Investment in Granite City Development LLC --- Lakemont Station Commons Project**

Dear Mr. Pellegrini:

This side letter (this \"**Side Letter**\") sets forth certain additional agreements between Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP, a Delaware limited partnership acting through its general partner, Lakeshore Capital Management LLC (\"**Investor**\" or \"**Lakeshore Capital**\"), and Granite City Development LLC, an Illinois limited liability company (\"**Developer**\"), regarding the investment by Investor in Developer in connection with the Lakemont Station Commons project, as more fully described herein. This Side Letter supplements the Operating Agreement (as defined below) and is entered into for good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged.

**[1. Recitals and Background]{.underline}**

**A.** Developer is an Illinois limited liability company formed on March 14, 2019, with its principal office located at 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200, Chicago, Illinois 60607, and is managed by Marcus J. Pellegrini, who serves as Manager and Chief Executive Officer of Developer.

**B.** Investor is a Delaware limited partnership with its principal office located at 200 East Randolph Street, Suite 3100, Chicago, Illinois 60601, acting through its general partner, Lakeshore Capital Management LLC. Richard \"Rick\" Dominguez serves as Managing Partner of Lakeshore Capital Management LLC.

**C.** Investor holds a twenty-five percent (25%) membership interest in Developer pursuant to the Amended and Restated Operating Agreement of Granite City Development LLC, dated as of September 8, 2023 (as amended from time to time, the \"**Operating Agreement**\"). The current ownership structure of Developer is as follows:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Member**                               **Membership Interest**
  ---------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Pellegrini Family Trust                  60%

  Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP   25%

  TransitWorks Equity Group LLC            15%
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

**D.** Developer has been selected by the City of Lakemont, Illinois (the \"**City**\") through a competitive Request for Proposals process to develop the Lakemont Station Commons project (the \"**Project**\"), a mixed-use transit-oriented development situated on an approximately 12.7-acre site located at 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, Illinois 60048 (Lake County Parcel IDs: 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017), adjacent to the Lakemont Central Metra commuter rail station.

**E.** The total estimated Project cost is approximately Four Hundred Twelve Million Dollars (\$412,000,000), with total hard construction costs of approximately Three Hundred Forty Million Dollars (\$340,000,000) across four development phases. City Council Resolution R-2025-017, adopted February 18, 2025, conditionally approved the Project, and a negotiated term sheet dated January 22, 2025 (the \"**Term Sheet**\") was executed by Marcus J. Pellegrini on behalf of Developer and City Manager Gerald T. Fong on behalf of the City. Developer expects to enter into a definitive Development Agreement with the City, with a target execution date of June 2025, contingent upon rezoning of the Project site from M-2 (General Manufacturing) to PD-TOD (Planned Development --- Transit-Oriented District).

**F.** Investor\'s total equity commitment to the Project is Fifty-One Million Dollars (\$51,000,000), representing a portion of the total committed equity of Eighty-Five Million Dollars (\$85,000,000), with the balance of Thirty-Four Million Dollars (\$34,000,000) to be contributed by the Pellegrini Family Trust.

**G.** The purpose of this Side Letter is to set forth certain additional agreements between Investor and Developer regarding consent rights, anti-dilution protections, minimum return requirements, and governance matters in connection with the Project, supplementing the Operating Agreement. In the event of any conflict between this Side Letter and the Operating Agreement with respect to matters related to the Project, this Side Letter shall control.

**[2. Definitions]{.underline}**

As used in this Side Letter, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below. Capitalized terms used but not defined herein shall have the meanings ascribed to them in the Operating Agreement.

**\"Change of Control\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 5 of this Side Letter.

**\"City\"** means the City of Lakemont, Illinois, a municipal corporation and home-rule unit of local government.

**\"Construction Lender\"** means First Prairie National Bank, located at 135 South LaSalle Street, Chicago, Illinois 60603 (Relationship Manager: Sandra L. Chen), or any successor construction lender approved in accordance with the Operating Agreement and this Side Letter.

**\"Development Agreement\"** means the development agreement to be entered into between Developer and the City for the Lakemont Station Commons Project, together with all exhibits, schedules, and ancillary agreements thereto.

**\"Material Amendment\"** means any amendment, modification, or waiver of the Development Agreement that: (i) reduces or restructures any public incentive, including the tax increment financing (\"TIF\") reimbursement currently capped at Forty-Eight Million Dollars (\$48,000,000), the land write-down of Nine Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$9,200,000), any property tax abatement, permit fee waivers of One Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$1,400,000), or the City infrastructure commitment of Twelve Million Five Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$12,500,000); (ii) accelerates or extends any phase commencement or completion deadline by more than six (6) months; (iii) increases Developer\'s financial obligations, including on-site infrastructure obligations currently estimated at Eight Million Three Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$8,300,000) or environmental remediation costs currently estimated at Three Million One Hundred Forty Thousand Dollars (\$3,140,000); (iv) modifies affordable housing requirements, currently set at twenty percent (20%) of total residential units at or below sixty percent (60%) of area median income; (v) modifies assignment or change-of-control restrictions applicable to Developer; or (vi) alters any reversionary interest, clawback mechanism, or similar provision benefiting the City.

**\"Minimum IRR\"** means a fourteen percent (14%) internal rate of return on Investor\'s equity commitment, calculated on a net-of-fees, pre-tax basis.

**\"Operating Agreement\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 1(C) above.

**\"Pellegrini Interest\"** means the aggregate direct and indirect membership interest in Developer held by Marcus J. Pellegrini and/or the Pellegrini Family Trust, whether held of record or beneficially.

**\"Project\"** means the Lakemont Station Commons mixed-use transit-oriented development, as described in Section 1(D) above, including all phases thereof.

**[3. Investment Commitment and Minimum Return]{.underline}**

**3.1** Investor confirms its commitment to invest Fifty-One Million Dollars (\$51,000,000) in Developer equity for the Project, subject to and in accordance with the terms of the Operating Agreement, this Side Letter, and the capital call procedures set forth in the Operating Agreement.

**3.2** Developer acknowledges and agrees that the Minimum IRR on all capital contributions made by Investor is a material inducement to Investor\'s participation in the Project. Developer shall use commercially reasonable efforts to structure and manage the Project so as to achieve or exceed the Minimum IRR.

**3.3** Developer shall provide Investor with quarterly unaudited financial statements within forty-five (45) days following each fiscal quarter-end and annual audited financial statements prepared by Clearmont Accounting Group LLP within ninety (90) days following each fiscal year-end, in each case demonstrating both projected and actual returns to Investor.

**3.4** If, at any time after Phase I Substantial Completion (the deadline for which is currently September 30, 2028), the projected IRR for Investor\'s investment falls below the Minimum IRR of fourteen percent (14%), Developer shall promptly notify Investor in writing and the parties shall meet within thirty (30) days of such notice to discuss restructuring options in good faith, including but not limited to: (a) reallocation of cash distributions in favor of Investor; (b) modification of capital contribution schedules for later phases; (c) adjustment to the promote and distribution waterfall structure under the Operating Agreement; or (d) acceleration of Investor\'s exit rights under the Operating Agreement or this Side Letter.

**3.5** Developer shall not, without Investor\'s prior written consent, agree to any term in the Development Agreement or any ancillary agreement with the City that would materially and adversely affect Investor\'s ability to achieve the Minimum IRR, including without limitation the acceptance of obligations or conditions that would materially increase total Project costs beyond the current estimated Total Project Cost of Four Hundred Twelve Million Dollars (\$412,000,000).

**[4. Consent Rights over Development Agreement]{.underline}**

**4.1** *Review of Development Agreement.* Investor shall have the right to review and approve the final form of the Development Agreement prior to its execution by Developer. Developer shall provide Investor with a substantially final draft of the Development Agreement at least fifteen (15) business days prior to the anticipated execution date. Developer shall consider in good faith any comments provided by Investor during such review period.

**4.2** *Material Amendment Consent.* Following execution of the Development Agreement, Investor\'s prior written consent shall be required for any Material Amendment. Such consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed, and Investor shall respond in writing within ten (10) business days of receipt of a written request for consent accompanied by supporting documentation reasonably describing the proposed amendment and its anticipated effects on the Project and Investor\'s returns.

**4.3** *Specific Consent Requirements.* Without limiting the generality of Section 4.2, Investor\'s prior written consent shall be required for any of the following:

> \(a\) Any change to the TIF reimbursement cap (currently Forty-Eight Million Dollars (\$48,000,000)) or to the categories of TIF-eligible costs;
>
> \(b\) Any change to the property tax abatement schedule (currently Years 1--4: seventy-five percent (75%); Years 5--8: fifty percent (50%); Years 9--12: twenty-five percent (25%), applicable to Phase I only);
>
> \(c\) Any modification to phasing timelines that would extend overall Project completion beyond calendar year 2035;
>
> \(d\) Any acceptance of environmental remediation obligations exceeding the current estimate of Three Million One Hundred Forty Thousand Dollars (\$3,140,000) without a corresponding increase in TIF reimbursement or direct City funding sufficient to offset such excess;
>
> \(e\) Any modification to performance bond or letter of credit requirements (currently Fifteen Million Dollars (\$15,000,000) for Phase I, with amounts for later phases to be established in the Development Agreement);
>
> \(f\) Any amendment to assignment or change-of-control provisions in the Development Agreement.

**4.4** *Enhanced Consent --- Pellegrini Interest Threshold.* If at any time the Pellegrini Interest falls below fifty-one percent (51%) of the total membership interests in Developer (whether through dilution, transfer, or otherwise), Investor shall have the additional right to consent to any amendment, modification, or waiver of the Development Agreement, and not merely those constituting Material Amendments. The parties acknowledge that this fifty-one percent (51%) threshold is intended to provide Investor with enhanced governance protections at a level above the assignment restriction threshold contemplated in the Term Sheet (which requires Pellegrini to hold at least fifty percent (50%) controlling interest in Developer), and that additional capital raises for later phases of the Project are anticipated and may affect the Pellegrini Interest. The mechanism set forth in Section 5 below is intended to address the interplay among Investor\'s participation rights, future capital needs, and the Pellegrini Interest threshold.

**[5. Anti-Dilution Protections and Change of Control]{.underline}**

**5.1** *Anti-Dilution Rights.* If Developer proposes to issue additional membership interests or admit new members (including in connection with the approximately Fifty-Eight Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$58,400,000) in additional debt, mezzanine financing, or equity anticipated for later phases of the Project), Investor shall have the following rights:

> \(a\) *Preemptive Right.* Investor shall have the right, but not the obligation, to participate on a pro rata basis in any new equity issuance in order to maintain its twenty-five percent (25%) membership interest percentage. Developer shall provide Investor with written notice of any proposed issuance at least thirty (30) days prior to the anticipated closing thereof, together with all material terms and conditions of the proposed issuance. Investor shall have twenty (20) business days from receipt of such notice to elect to exercise its preemptive right.
>
> \(b\) *Anti-Dilution Adjustment.* In the event Investor does not exercise its preemptive right in full and Investor\'s percentage membership interest is diluted below twenty-five percent (25%), Investor shall receive a preferential return adjustment under the distribution waterfall set forth in the Operating Agreement, calculated to preserve the Minimum IRR of fourteen percent (14%) as applied to all capital contributions made by Investor, taking into account the effect of dilution on Investor\'s share of Project cash flows.
>
> \(c\) *Information Rights.* Developer shall provide Investor with copies of all term sheets, commitment letters, and proposed equity documentation for any new capital raise at least fifteen (15) business days prior to execution thereof.

**5.2** *Definition of Change of Control.* A \"**Change of Control**\" shall be deemed to occur upon any of the following events: (i) the Pellegrini Interest falls below fifty-one percent (51%) of the total membership interests in Developer; (ii) Marcus J. Pellegrini ceases to serve as Manager and Chief Executive Officer of Developer and is not replaced by a person reasonably approved by Investor within sixty (60) days following such cessation; (iii) any single person or entity (other than the Pellegrini Family Trust, Investor, or TransitWorks Equity Group LLC) acquires twenty-five percent (25%) or more of the membership interests in Developer; or (iv) Developer merges with, consolidates into, or is acquired by another entity.

**5.3** *Acknowledgment of Future Capital Needs.* The parties acknowledge that the approximately Fifty-Eight Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$58,400,000) in additional mezzanine and debt financing anticipated for later phases of the Project may require equity contributions, equity conversion features, or other structural elements that could have the effect of diluting the Pellegrini Interest below both the fifty-one percent (51%) threshold set forth in this Side Letter and the fifty percent (50%) assignment restriction threshold contemplated in the Term Sheet. In light of this anticipated dynamic, the parties agree to work together in good faith to structure the Development Agreement\'s assignment and change-of-control provisions in a manner that is compatible with Investor\'s rights under this Side Letter and Developer\'s anticipated future capital needs, including by negotiating with the City for a broader \"permitted transfer\" carve-out that accommodates internal transfers among existing members and capital raises approved by both Investor and the City.

**5.4** *Consequences of Change of Control.* Upon the occurrence of a Change of Control without Investor\'s prior written consent, Investor may elect, by written notice to Developer delivered within ninety (90) days of becoming aware of such Change of Control, to exercise one of the following remedies:

> \(a\) *Repurchase Right.* Investor may require Developer to repurchase Investor\'s entire membership interest at a price equal to the greater of (x) Investor\'s unreturned capital contributions plus the Minimum IRR accrued thereon to the date of repurchase, or (y) the fair market value of Investor\'s membership interest as determined by an independent MAI-certified appraiser mutually selected by the parties (or, if the parties cannot agree, appointed in accordance with the arbitration provisions of Section 10); or
>
> \(b\) *Management Right.* Investor may exercise a right to assume the role of Manager of Developer or to appoint a co-Manager of Developer alongside Marcus J. Pellegrini (or his successor), in each case subject to obtaining any required consent from the City under the Development Agreement and the Construction Lender under the applicable loan documents.

**[6. Reporting and Information Rights]{.underline}**

Developer shall provide Investor with the following information and access:

> \(a\) Monthly construction progress reports for each active phase of the Project, delivered within fifteen (15) days following the end of each calendar month;
>
> \(b\) Quarterly unaudited financial statements of Developer, delivered within forty-five (45) days following the end of each fiscal quarter;
>
> \(c\) Annual audited financial statements of Developer prepared by Clearmont Accounting Group LLP, delivered within ninety (90) days following the end of each fiscal year;
>
> \(d\) Copies of all material correspondence between Developer and the City of Lakemont, First Prairie National Bank, and the Illinois Environmental Protection Agency (related to environmental remediation under the Voluntary Site Remediation Program), promptly upon transmission or receipt;
>
> \(e\) Prompt written notice (within five (5) business days of Developer becoming aware thereof) of any default or alleged default under the Development Agreement, any construction loan agreement, or any environmental compliance matter affecting the Project;
>
> \(f\) Copies of all TIF reimbursement requests submitted to the City and all City approvals or denials thereof, promptly upon submission or receipt.

Investor shall have reasonable access to the books, records, and accounts of Developer relating to the Project and to the Project site during normal business hours upon not fewer than three (3) business days\' advance written notice.

**[7. Restrictions on Developer Actions Without Investor Consent]{.underline}**

In addition to the Material Amendment consent rights set forth in Section 4, Developer shall not, without Investor\'s prior written consent, take any of the following actions:

> \(a\) Incur Project-level indebtedness in excess of Two Hundred Ten Million Dollars (\$210,000,000) (representing the Construction Lender facility amount) plus Fifty-Eight Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$58,400,000) (representing anticipated additional debt and mezzanine financing for later phases), or Two Hundred Sixty-Eight Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$268,400,000) in the aggregate;
>
> \(b\) Grant any lien, mortgage, security interest, or encumbrance on any portion of the Project site (Parcel IDs: 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017) other than the Construction Lender\'s mortgage, standard permitted encumbrances as defined in the Operating Agreement, and liens required by the Development Agreement;
>
> \(c\) Modify the Project phasing schedule in a manner that would defer Phase I Substantial Completion beyond September 30, 2028 (as such date may be extended by events of force majeure as defined in the Operating Agreement or the Development Agreement) or defer overall Project completion beyond calendar year 2035;
>
> \(d\) Agree to any clawback, reversionary interest, right of re-entry, repurchase option, or similar mechanism in the Development Agreement or any ancillary agreement without Investor\'s consent, it being acknowledged that City Council Resolution R-2025-017, Condition No. 8, contemplates such a mechanism and that the parties must negotiate terms compatible with the Construction Lender\'s requirements and Investor\'s interests;
>
> \(e\) Waive any material right of Developer under the Development Agreement, including without limitation any termination right, force majeure extension right, or right to receive public incentives.

**[8. Coordination with Development Agreement and Construction Loan]{.underline}**

**8.1** Developer acknowledges that the Development Agreement must be in form and substance reasonably acceptable to First Prairie National Bank, as reflected in the Construction Lender\'s preliminary term sheet for a Two Hundred Ten Million Dollars (\$210,000,000) construction facility for Phase I of the Project.

**8.2** Developer shall keep Investor informed on a current basis of all Construction Lender requirements that affect or may affect Investor\'s rights under this Side Letter or the Operating Agreement, including without limitation any Construction Lender restrictions on transfers of membership interests in Developer, any requirements for guarantees or equity maintenance covenants, and any conditions to draw-downs that may affect Project timing or cost.

**8.3** The parties acknowledge that the Development Agreement\'s assignment and change-of-control restrictions (currently contemplated in the Term Sheet as requiring City consent for any assignment except to an entity in which Marcus J. Pellegrini holds at least fifty percent (50%) controlling interest) must be harmonized with: (a) Investor\'s anti-dilution, consent, and governance rights under this Side Letter; (b) anticipated future capital raises for later phases of the Project; and (c) the Construction Lender\'s anticipated assignment, change-of-control, and equity maintenance covenants. Developer commits to negotiate Development Agreement assignment and change-of-control provisions that include: (i) carve-outs for internal transfers of membership interests among existing members of Developer; (ii) carve-outs for capital raises that have been approved by both Investor and the City; (iii) a \"Key Person\" provision requiring Marcus J. Pellegrini\'s active and material involvement as Manager and Chief Executive Officer of Developer, as an alternative or supplement to rigid percentage-of-interest thresholds; and (iv) a reasonable cure period (not less than sixty (60) days) before any assignment default is triggered under the Development Agreement.

**8.4** Developer shall provide Investor with the final form of the Development Agreement and the executed Construction Lender commitment letter for Investor\'s review prior to closing on the acquisition of the Project site.

**[9. Term and Termination]{.underline}**

This Side Letter shall be effective as of the date first written above and shall remain in full force and effect until the earliest of: (a) the date on which Investor ceases to hold any membership interest in Developer; (b) the date of final completion of all phases of the Project and the distribution of all proceeds to Investor in accordance with the Operating Agreement and this Side Letter; or (c) the date of mutual written agreement of the parties to terminate this Side Letter. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sections 3.2 through 3.5 (Minimum IRR), Section 5.4 (Change of Control consequences), and Section 10 (Miscellaneous Provisions, including without limitation the dispute resolution, confidentiality, and governing law provisions) shall survive any termination of this Side Letter.

**[10. Miscellaneous Provisions]{.underline}**

**10.1** *Governing Law.* This Side Letter shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois, without regard to conflict-of-law principles.

**10.2** *Dispute Resolution.* Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Side Letter shall be resolved in a manner consistent with the dispute resolution provisions of the Operating Agreement. To the extent not addressed therein, the parties agree to first attempt resolution through good faith negotiation for a period of thirty (30) days, then through mediation administered by the American Arbitration Association, and if mediation is unsuccessful within sixty (60) days of initiation, then through binding arbitration under the AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules. The venue for any mediation or arbitration shall be Cook County, Illinois.

**10.3** *Notices.* All notices, requests, demands, and other communications under this Side Letter shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given when delivered personally, sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or transmitted by email (with confirmation of receipt), addressed as follows:

> If to Investor: Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP 200 East Randolph Street, Suite 3100 Chicago, Illinois 60601
>
> Attention: Richard Dominguez, Managing Partner
>
> If to Developer: Granite City Development LLC 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200 Chicago, Illinois 60607
>
> Attention: Marcus J. Pellegrini, Manager/CEO
>
> With a copy to: Thornfield & Pratt LLP 311 South Wacker Drive, Suite 5400 Chicago, Illinois 60606
>
> Attention: Catherine M. Albrecht

**10.4** *Confidentiality.* This Side Letter and its terms are confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party; *provided*, that either party may disclose this Side Letter and its terms to the City of Lakemont, First Prairie National Bank, and their respective legal counsel, accountants, and advisors to the extent reasonably necessary for the negotiation, execution, and performance of the Development Agreement, the construction loan, and related Project documentation.

**10.5** *Conflict with Operating Agreement.* In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between the provisions of this Side Letter and the provisions of the Operating Agreement with respect to matters related to the Project, the provisions of this Side Letter shall control.

**10.6** *Amendments.* This Side Letter may not be amended, modified, or supplemented except by a written instrument executed by both Investor and Developer.

**10.7** *Counterparts.* This Side Letter may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures transmitted by electronic means (including PDF and DocuSign) shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

**10.8** *Entire Agreement.* This Side Letter, together with the Operating Agreement, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, warranties, and understandings relating thereto. This Side Letter supplements, but does not supersede, the Operating Agreement except as expressly provided herein.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Side Letter as of the date first written above.

**LAKESHORE CAPITAL PARTNERS FUND III LP**

By: Lakeshore Capital Management LLC, its General Partner

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Richard \"Rick\" Dominguez

Title: Managing Partner

Date: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

**ACKNOWLEDGED AND AGREED:**

**GRANITE CITY DEVELOPMENT LLC**
