result:
**FOR DISCUSSION PURPOSES --- NON-BINDING**

**NEGOTIATED TERM SHEET**

**LAKEMONT STATION COMMONS** **MIXED-USE TRANSIT-ORIENTED DEVELOPMENT**

January 22, 2025

**Between:**

**Granite City Development LLC**, an Illinois limited liability company (\"Developer\")

**and**

**City of Lakemont, Illinois**, an Illinois home rule municipality (\"City\")

This Term Sheet summarizes the principal business terms negotiated between Developer and City for the development of the Lakemont Station Commons project (the \"Project\"). This Term Sheet is non-binding and is intended solely to facilitate the preparation of a definitive Development Agreement (the \"Agreement\"). Neither party shall have any binding obligation unless and until a definitive Agreement is executed and delivered by both parties and approved by the Lakemont City Council.

Developer was selected by the City through a competitive Request for Proposals process (RFP No. 2024-CD-018) conducted by the City\'s Community Development Department during 2024. Developer\'s proposal was evaluated and recommended for selection by the evaluation committee and thereafter by the City Manager. This Term Sheet reflects the principal terms negotiated between the parties following Developer\'s selection, subject to anticipated City Council approval. The terms set forth herein are further subject to the adoption by the City Council of a resolution authorizing the negotiation and execution of the definitive Agreement, which the parties anticipate will be considered at the City Council meeting scheduled for February 18, 2025.

**[Section 1: Parties]{.underline}**

**1.1 Developer**

> • **Entity Name:** Granite City Development LLC
>
> • **Jurisdiction:** Illinois limited liability company, formed March 14, 2019
>
> • **Principal Office:** 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200, Chicago, IL 60607
>
> • **Manager/CEO:** Marcus J. Pellegrini
>
> • **Developer\'s Counsel:** Thornfield & Pratt LLP (Catherine M. Albrecht, lead partner), 311 South Wacker Drive, Suite 5400, Chicago, IL 60606

**1.2 Ownership of Developer**

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Member**                               **Interest**
  ---------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Pellegrini Family Trust                  60%

  Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP   25%

  TransitWorks Equity Group LLC            15%
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Marcus J. Pellegrini serves as Trustee of the Pellegrini Family Trust and as Manager and CEO of the Developer entity. TransitWorks Equity Group LLC is a transit-oriented development investment vehicle managed by former public transit officials and institutional investors.

**1.3 City**

> • **Entity Name:** City of Lakemont, Illinois
>
> • **Status:** Home rule municipality; approximate population 87,000
>
> • **City Manager:** Gerald T. Fong
>
> • **Corporation Counsel:** Rebecca A. Sinclair, Esq.
>
> • **City\'s Outside Counsel:** Ashford, Keene & Mulvaney LLP (Jonathan Ashford, partner), 150 North Michigan Avenue, Suite 3200, Chicago, IL 60601

**[Section 2: The Site]{.underline}**

> • **Address:** 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, IL 60048
>
> • **Parcel Identification Numbers:**
>
> • 12-24-300-015
>
> • 12-24-300-016
>
> • 12-24-300-017

The three parcels are to be consolidated into a single parcel prior to or concurrent with land conveyance to Developer.

> • **Total Acreage:** Approximately 12.7 acres (553,212 square feet)
>
> • **Current Ownership:** City of Lakemont. The City acquired Parcels 12-24-300-015 and 12-24-300-016 through tax forfeiture proceedings in 2016, and acquired Parcel 12-24-300-017 through direct purchase in 2018.
>
> • **Current Zoning:** M-2 (General Manufacturing)
>
> • **Required Rezoning:** PD-TOD (Planned Development--Transit Oriented District). Rezoning of the Site from M-2 to PD-TOD shall be completed and effective prior to execution of the definitive Agreement.
>
> • **Adjacency:** The Site is located directly adjacent to the new Lakemont Central Metra station, which commenced service in October 2023 and serves the Milwaukee District/North Line.
>
> • **Prior Use:** The Site is the former location of the Lakemont Ironworks manufacturing campus, which ceased operations in 2009. Existing structures on the Site have been demolished; the Site is currently vacant and unimproved.
>
> • **Appraisal:** The Site has been appraised at **\$18,400,000** (as-is market value) by Crossland Appraisal Services, in an appraisal report dated January 15, 2025. The appraisal was commissioned by the City and conducted in accordance with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP). The as-is value reflects the environmental conditions described below.
>
> • **Environmental Status:** A Phase II Environmental Site Assessment was completed by Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC (report dated November 8, 2024). The Phase II ESA identified petroleum hydrocarbon contamination (BTEX compounds) in soil and groundwater on Parcel 12-24-300-016, affecting approximately 1.8 acres. Low-level metals contamination was identified in shallow soil on Parcel 12-24-300-017. Remediation is required for both parcels. Further details regarding environmental conditions and remediation are set forth in Section 6 below.

**[Section 3: The Project]{.underline}**

> • **Project Name:** Lakemont Station Commons
>
> • **Total Gross Building Area:** Approximately 1,620,000 square feet across four phases
>
> • **Total Estimated Hard Construction Cost:** \$340,000,000
>
> • **Total Project Cost (including soft costs, land, financing costs):** Approximately \$412,000,000

The Project consists of a mixed-use, transit-oriented development organized in four phases as described below.

**3.1 Phase I --- Residential Tower A and Retail Podium**

> • 320 residential units: 256 market-rate units and 64 affordable units restricted to households earning at or below 60% of Area Median Income (\"AMI\")
>
> • 45,000 square feet of ground-floor retail space
>
> • Structured parking garage: 480 spaces
>
> • Gross building area: approximately 420,000 square feet
>
> • Estimated hard construction cost: \$105,000,000
>
> • **Timeline:** Groundbreaking no later than March 1, 2026. Substantial Completion by September 30, 2028 (approximately 30-month construction period).

**3.2 Phase II --- Office Building and Retail**

> • 280,000 square feet of Class A office space
>
> • 18,000 square feet of retail space
>
> • Structured parking garage: 350 spaces
>
> • Gross building area: approximately 380,000 square feet
>
> • Estimated hard construction cost: \$76,000,000
>
> • **Timeline:** Commence construction within 12 months of Phase I Substantial Completion. Substantial Completion within 24 months of Phase II commencement.

**3.3 Phase III --- Residential Tower B and Hotel**

> • 210 residential units: 168 market-rate units and 42 affordable units restricted to households earning at or below 60% of AMI
>
> • 150-room select-service hotel (brand affiliation to be determined; Developer is in discussions with two national hospitality flags)
>
> • Gross building area: approximately 440,000 square feet
>
> • Estimated hard construction cost: \$88,000,000
>
> • **Timeline:** Commence construction within 12 months of Phase II Substantial Completion. Substantial Completion within 30 months of Phase III commencement.

**3.4 Phase IV --- Public Plaza, Community Center, and Parking Structure**

> • 2.5-acre public plaza with landscaping, hardscape, seating areas, and public art installations
>
> • 28,000-square-foot community center, to be designed in consultation with the City\'s Parks and Recreation Department and conveyed to the City upon completion at no additional cost
>
> • 600-space public parking structure serving the transit station, community center, and adjacent commercial uses
>
> • Gross building area: approximately 380,000 square feet (parking structure and community center combined)
>
> • Estimated hard construction cost: \$71,000,000
>
> • **Timeline:** Commence construction concurrently with Phase III. Substantial Completion within 24 months of Phase IV commencement.

**3.5 Summary Totals**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Component**                       **Total**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  Total Residential Units             530 (320 Phase I + 210 Phase III)

  Total Affordable Units              At least 100 affordable units across the Project, at or below 60% AMI

  Total Office Space                  280,000 sq ft

  Total Retail Space                  63,000 sq ft (45,000 Phase I + 18,000 Phase II)

  Hotel Rooms                         150 rooms

  Community Center                    28,000 sq ft

  Public Plaza                        2.5 acres

  Total Parking Spaces                1,430 spaces (480 + 350 + 600)

  Total Hard Construction Cost        \$340,000,000

  Total Project Cost                  Approximately \$412,000,000
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Project is designed as a cohesive, master-planned transit-oriented development that will serve as a catalyst for the revitalization of the Lakemont Central station area and the surrounding neighborhoods. The phased approach reflects the anticipated market absorption and financing timeline for each component.

**[Section 4: Land Conveyance]{.underline}**

> • The City shall convey the Site (as consolidated into a single parcel) to Developer by special warranty deed, following execution of the definitive Agreement and satisfaction of all conditions precedent to closing.
>
> • **Sale Price:** \$9,200,000, representing 50% of the appraised as-is market value of \$18,400,000, consistent with the City\'s economic development incentive policy for transit-oriented development projects. The difference between the appraised value and the sale price (\$9,200,000) constitutes a land write-down incentive.
>
> • **Payment:** The purchase price of \$9,200,000 shall be due and payable at closing. Developer may fund the purchase price from equity, construction loan proceeds, or a combination thereof.
>
> • **Title:** The City shall deliver marketable and insurable fee simple title to the Site, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, and exceptions, except for Permitted Exceptions to be identified and agreed upon in the definitive Agreement. The City shall provide a standard owner\'s title insurance policy issued by a nationally recognized title insurance company in the amount of the purchase price.
>
> • **Conditions Precedent to Closing:**
>
> \(i\) Execution of the definitive Agreement by both parties and approval by the City Council;
>
> \(ii\) Completion of rezoning of the Site from M-2 to PD-TOD;
>
> \(iii\) Completion or authorization of parcel consolidation of Parcels 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017;
>
> \(iv\) Illinois Environmental Protection Agency (\"IEPA\") approval of the Remedial Action Plan for Parcel 12-24-300-016;
>
> \(v\) Developer\'s demonstration of committed equity of at least \$85,000,000, in form and substance reasonably acceptable to the City; and
>
> \(vi\) Developer\'s posting of a \$15,000,000 performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit for Phase I, in form and substance reasonably acceptable to the City.
>
> • **Closing:** Closing shall occur within 30 days following the satisfaction or waiver of all conditions precedent. The parties\' target closing date is mid-2025, subject to the timing of rezoning, IEPA approvals, and the satisfaction of other conditions.

**[Section 5: Public Incentives]{.underline}**

**5.1 Land Write-Down**

> • Sale of the Site at 50% of appraised as-is market value: \$9,200,000 purchase price versus \$18,400,000 appraised value.
>
> • Incentive value: \$9,200,000.

**5.2 Tax Increment Financing (TIF)**

> • The Site is located within the **Lakemont Central TIF District**, established pursuant to City Ordinance No. 2019-42, with an expiration date of December 31, 2042.
>
> • The City shall pledge up to **\$48,000,000** in TIF increment over the remaining life of the Lakemont Central TIF District to reimburse Developer for eligible TIF costs incurred in connection with the Project. Eligible TIF costs shall include, without limitation: public infrastructure improvements, environmental remediation costs, parking structure construction costs, and other eligible project costs under the Illinois Tax Increment Allocation Redevelopment Act, 65 ILCS 5/11-74.4-1 et seq. (the \"TIF Act\").
>
> • TIF reimbursement shall be on a **pay-as-you-go** basis. Developer shall front all eligible costs and shall be reimbursed annually from available TIF increment as generated by the Project and other developments within the Lakemont Central TIF District. The City shall not issue TIF bonds or notes for the Project.
>
> • Estimated annual TIF increment at full Project buildout is approximately \$4,200,000 per year. This estimate is based on the equalized assessed valuations projected by the City\'s finance department and is subject to change based on actual assessed values, tax rates, and the performance of properties within the TIF District.
>
> • TIF reimbursement is **subject to the availability of increment**. The City does not guarantee that the full \$48,000,000 cap will be reached over the remaining life of the TIF District. Developer acknowledges that actual reimbursement will depend on the amount of increment generated and available in each year.

**5.3 Infrastructure Cost-Sharing**

> • **City Commitment:** The City shall fund **\$12,500,000** for off-site infrastructure improvements necessary to support the Project, including:
>
> • Road widening and streetscape improvements on Lakemont Station Boulevard between Oak Street and Maple Avenue
>
> • Installation of a new traffic signal at the Oak Street/Lakemont Station Boulevard intersection
>
> • Extension of the water main along Lakemont Station Boulevard to serve the Site
>
> • Sanitary sewer upgrade and capacity expansion in the Lakemont Central service area
>
> • **Developer Responsibility:** Developer shall fund **\$8,300,000** for on-site infrastructure improvements, including internal roadways, utilities, stormwater management, pedestrian pathways, and landscape improvements within the Site.
>
> • **Total Infrastructure Budget:** \$20,800,000 (\$12,500,000 City + \$8,300,000 Developer).
>
> • The City\'s infrastructure funding shall be appropriated and disbursed according to a schedule to be set forth in the definitive Agreement, coordinated with the phased construction timeline.

**5.4 Property Tax Abatement**

> • The City shall provide a 12-year declining property tax abatement on **Phase I improvements only**, as follows:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Period**                          **Abatement Level**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Years 1--4                          75% abatement

  Years 5--8                          50% abatement

  Years 9--12                         25% abatement

  Year 13 and thereafter              0% (full assessment)
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

> • The abatement shall apply to the incremental increase in assessed value attributable to the Phase I improvements (Residential Tower A and Retail Podium), measured from the pre-development base equalized assessed value.

**5.5 Permit Fee Waiver**

> • The City shall waive building permit fees for Phase I and Phase II of the Project.
>
> • Estimated waiver value: \$920,000 (Phase I) + \$480,000 (Phase II) = **\$1,400,000**.
>
> • No waiver of building permit fees is contemplated for Phase III or Phase IV.

**5.6 Summary of Public Incentives**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Incentive**                                **Value**
  -------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------
  Land Write-Down                              \$9,200,000

  TIF Reimbursement (up to)                    \$48,000,000

  City Infrastructure Funding                  \$12,500,000

  Property Tax Abatement (Phase I, 12 years)   To be calculated based on actual assessed values

  Permit Fee Waivers (Phases I & II)           \$1,400,000
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Section 6: Environmental Matters]{.underline}**

> • **Phase II ESA:** A Phase II Environmental Site Assessment was completed by Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC, report dated November 8, 2024, covering all three parcels comprising the Site. The key findings are summarized below:
>
> **Parcel 12-24-300-015:** No contaminants of concern detected above applicable IEPA residential or commercial remediation objectives. No further action required.
>
> **Parcel 12-24-300-016:** Petroleum hydrocarbon contamination (benzene, toluene, ethylbenzene, and xylenes --- BTEX compounds) detected in soil and shallow groundwater across approximately 1.8 acres, concentrated in the northwestern portion of the parcel. Remediation is required under the IEPA Voluntary Site Remediation Program (\"VSRP\"). Estimated remediation cost: \$2,800,000. Estimated remediation timeline: 14--18 months from IEPA approval of the Remedial Action Plan. A No Further Remediation (\"NFR\") letter from IEPA will be required prior to construction of improvements on the affected area.
>
> **Parcel 12-24-300-017:** Low-level metals contamination (primarily lead and chromium) detected in shallow soil at concentrations above IEPA residential remediation objectives but below IEPA commercial/industrial remediation objectives. An engineered barrier consisting of a clean fill cap and vapor barrier is required for residential use. Estimated cost: \$340,000.
>
> • **Total Estimated Environmental Remediation Costs:** \$3,140,000 (\$2,800,000 for Parcel 016 + \$340,000 for Parcel 017).
>
> • **Indemnification:** The City shall indemnify and hold harmless the Developer, its members, officers, employees, agents, contractors, and successors from and against all claims, demands, losses, costs, damages, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising from or related to pre-existing environmental conditions on the City-owned parcels (Parcels 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017).
>
> • Environmental remediation costs are referenced in Section 5.2 above as an eligible TIF-reimbursable cost.
>
> • IEPA approval of the Remedial Action Plan for Parcel 12-24-300-016 is a condition precedent to land conveyance, as set forth in Section 4 above.

**[Section 7: Project Financing]{.underline}**

Developer\'s preliminary sources and uses for the Project are summarized as follows:

**Sources of Funds:**

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Source**                                                   **Amount**
  ------------------------------------------------------------ -----------------------------------
  Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP --- Equity            \$51,000,000

  Pellegrini Family Trust --- Equity                           \$34,000,000

  **Total Committed Equity**                                   **\$85,000,000**

  Construction Loan --- First Prairie National Bank            \$210,000,000

  TIF Reimbursement (over time, up to)                         \$48,000,000

  Land Discount Value                                          \$9,200,000

  Permit Fee Waivers                                           \$1,400,000

  Additional Debt/Mezzanine (later phases, to be determined)   \$58,400,000

  **Total Sources**                                            **\$412,000,000**
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> • **Construction Loan:** First Prairie National Bank has provided a preliminary commitment of \$210,000,000 for the Project\'s construction financing. The commitment is subject to execution of the definitive Agreement in a form acceptable to the lender, satisfactory completion of due diligence, and customary loan conditions. Developer anticipates the construction loan will be drawn in tranches corresponding to each phase of construction.
>
> • **Equity Demonstration:** Developer shall demonstrate committed equity of at least \$85,000,000 within 60 days of City Council approval of the definitive Agreement, in the form of audited account statements, executed subscription agreements, or other evidence reasonably acceptable to the City.
>
> • **Developer Financial Capacity:** Developer\'s audited financials are prepared by Clearmont Accounting Group LLP. The Developer entity\'s net worth is approximately \$185,000,000, inclusive of the value of other projects in the Developer\'s portfolio. Developer\'s bonding capacity through Ridgepoint Surety Corporation is up to \$75,000,000.
>
> • **Additional Financing for Later Phases:** Approximately \$58,400,000 in additional debt or mezzanine financing will be required for Phases II through IV. Developer anticipates sourcing this capital through a combination of mezzanine loans, institutional co-investment, and cash flow from completed earlier phases. The structure and terms of this additional financing have not yet been determined and will be finalized as the Project progresses.

**[Section 8: Performance Security]{.underline}**

> • Developer shall post a **\$15,000,000 performance bond** (to be issued by Ridgepoint Surety Corporation, or another surety company reasonably acceptable to the City) **or an irrevocable letter of credit** issued by a nationally recognized financial institution, as a condition precedent to land conveyance.
>
> • The performance bond or letter of credit shall guarantee Developer\'s obligation to commence and substantially complete Phase I of the Project in accordance with the approved plans, specifications, and timeline set forth in the definitive Agreement.
>
> • The performance bond or letter of credit shall be maintained continuously from the date of land conveyance until Phase I Substantial Completion, as evidenced by issuance of a temporary or final certificate of occupancy by the City. Upon Phase I Substantial Completion, the performance bond or letter of credit shall be released and returned to Developer within 30 days following written request and verification of Substantial Completion by the City.
>
> • The amount, form, conditions, and draw mechanics of the performance bond or letter of credit shall be set forth in greater detail in the definitive Agreement.

**[Section 9: Community Benefits]{.underline}**

> • **Local Hiring:** Developer shall use commercially reasonable efforts to achieve a goal of **35% Lakemont residents** for construction workforce employment across all phases of the Project. This goal shall be measured on a cumulative project-wide basis and shall encompass both direct employees and employees of general contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers performing construction work on the Site.
>
> • **Reporting:** Developer shall submit quarterly reports to the City\'s Community Development Department (Director: Paul R. Vasquez) documenting local hiring percentages, including workforce data by trade category, residency information, and total labor hours. Reports shall be submitted within 30 days following the end of each calendar quarter during which active construction is occurring.
>
> • **Good-Faith Compliance:** Failure to achieve the 35% local hiring goal in any given quarter or on a cumulative basis shall not, in and of itself, constitute a default under the Agreement; provided, however, that Developer must demonstrate good-faith efforts to achieve the goal. Such efforts shall include, at minimum, posting job openings with the City\'s workforce development partners, attending local job fairs, and providing reasonable notice of hiring opportunities to the Community Development Department.
>
> • Additional community benefit obligations, if any, shall be set forth in the definitive Agreement.

**[Section 10: Assignment and Change of Control]{.underline}**

> • Developer **may not assign** the Agreement or any interest therein, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the City, which consent may be withheld in the City\'s reasonable discretion.
>
> • **Permitted Assignment:** Notwithstanding the foregoing, Developer may assign the Agreement without the City\'s prior written consent to an entity in which **Marcus J. Pellegrini (or the Pellegrini Family Trust) holds at least a 50% controlling interest** and which entity expressly assumes in writing all of Developer\'s obligations under the Agreement. Developer shall provide the City with written notice of any such permitted assignment, together with a copy of the assignment and assumption agreement, within 15 days of the effective date of such assignment.
>
> • Any purported assignment in violation of this provision shall be void and of no force or effect and shall constitute a material default under the Agreement.
>
> • For purposes of this Section 10, the term \"assignment\" shall include any direct or indirect transfer, conveyance, pledge, hypothecation, or other disposition of the Agreement or any interest therein, whether voluntary or involuntary, by operation of law or otherwise.
>
> • The parties acknowledge that the City\'s selection of Developer was based, in substantial part, on the qualifications, experience, and financial capacity of Marcus J. Pellegrini and his affiliated entities, and that the assignment restriction set forth herein is a material inducement for the City\'s willingness to proceed with the Project.

**[Section 11: Construction Standards and Requirements]{.underline}**

**11.1 Prevailing Wage**

> • All construction performed on the Project shall comply with the Illinois Prevailing Wage Act, 820 ILCS 130/0.01 et seq. Developer shall include prevailing wage requirements in all contracts and subcontracts for construction work performed in connection with the Project on the Site.
>
> • Developer shall maintain certified payroll records for all construction workers employed on the Project and shall make such records available to the City and to the Illinois Department of Labor upon request. Developer shall require all contractors and subcontractors to maintain and submit certified payroll records in accordance with applicable law and the requirements of the definitive Agreement.
>
> • Developer shall post prevailing wage rate schedules at the construction site in accordance with the Illinois Prevailing Wage Act and shall update such postings as revised schedules are issued by the Illinois Department of Labor.

**11.2 LEED Certification**

> • All buildings within the Project shall be designed and constructed to achieve **LEED Gold certification** (or an equivalent green building certification as may be approved by the City in writing) as administered by the U.S. Green Building Council.
>
> • Developer shall provide evidence of LEED registration for each building with the U.S. Green Building Council prior to the commencement of construction of the applicable building.
>
> • The specific LEED rating system applicable to each building component (e.g., LEED BD+C: New Construction, LEED BD+C: Hospitality, LEED ND) shall be identified in the definitive Agreement. Developer shall submit documentation of LEED certification status to the City upon receipt of certification or, in the event certification has not been achieved, a report detailing the status of the certification process and any deficiencies.

**11.3 Insurance**

> • Developer shall maintain, or cause its general contractor to maintain, throughout the construction period for each phase of the Project, the following minimum insurance coverages:
>
> • **Commercial General Liability (CGL):** \$10,000,000 per occurrence and \$20,000,000 in the aggregate, including coverage for premises/operations, products/completed operations, contractual liability, and broad-form property damage.
>
> • **Umbrella/Excess Liability:** \$25,000,000 per occurrence and in the aggregate, in excess of the CGL policy.
>
> • **Builder\'s Risk:** Full replacement value of each phase under construction, written on an all-risk basis including coverage for fire, windstorm, vandalism, theft, flood, and earthquake (with sub-limits as appropriate).
>
> • **Workers\' Compensation:** Statutory limits as required by Illinois law.
>
> • **Automobile Liability:** \$5,000,000 combined single limit.
>
> • The City of Lakemont shall be named as an additional insured on the CGL and umbrella/excess liability policies. Certificates of insurance evidencing the required coverages shall be provided to the City prior to land conveyance and shall be updated annually and upon renewal of any policy. All policies shall provide for at least 30 days\' prior written notice to the City of cancellation, non-renewal, or material modification.

**[Section 12: Affordable Housing]{.underline}**

> • Developer shall include **at least 100 affordable units** across the Project, with rents restricted to households earning at or below 60% of Area Median Income (\"AMI\") as published annually by the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development for the Chicago-Naperville-Elgin, IL-IN-WI Metropolitan Statistical Area.
>
> • The planned affordable unit breakdown is as follows:
>
> • **Phase I (Residential Tower A):** 64 affordable units out of 320 total residential units
>
> • **Phase III (Residential Tower B):** 42 affordable units out of 210 total residential units
>
> • **Project Total:** 106 affordable units out of 530 total residential units
>
> • Affordable units shall be distributed proportionally across unit types (studio, one-bedroom, and two-bedroom) and shall not be segregated or concentrated on particular floors or in separate wings of the buildings. Affordable units shall be comparable in design, finishes, and amenity access to market-rate units within the same building, subject to reasonable variations consistent with industry practice.
>
> • Affordability restrictions shall remain in effect for a minimum period of **30 years** from the date of issuance of the certificate of occupancy for the applicable phase. The affordability restrictions shall be memorialized by a restrictive covenant recorded against the applicable parcel and running with the land, in a form to be set forth in the definitive Agreement.
>
> • Developer shall comply with the City\'s Affordable Housing Compliance Program and shall provide annual income certification documentation to the City\'s Community Development Department for all affordable units throughout the affordability restriction period.

**[Section 13: Phasing and Timelines]{.underline}**

The Project shall be constructed in four phases in accordance with the following schedule:

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Phase**               **Commencement Deadline**                             **Substantial Completion Deadline**
  ----------------------- ----------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------
  Phase I                 Groundbreaking no later than March 1, 2026            September 30, 2028

  Phase II                Within 12 months of Phase I Substantial Completion    Within 24 months of Phase II commencement

  Phase III               Within 12 months of Phase II Substantial Completion   Within 30 months of Phase III commencement

  Phase IV                Concurrent with Phase III commencement                Within 24 months of Phase IV commencement
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> • \"Substantial Completion\" shall be defined in the definitive Agreement. For purposes of this Term Sheet, Substantial Completion generally means the completion of construction of the applicable phase to the point where the building or improvements can be occupied and used for their intended purpose, as evidenced by the issuance of a temporary or final certificate of occupancy by the City\'s Building Department.
>
> • The commencement deadline for each subsequent phase is measured from the Substantial Completion date of the immediately preceding phase, as may be extended by applicable force majeure events under Section 14. For purposes of clarity, if Phase I Substantial Completion occurs on September 30, 2028, then Phase II must commence no later than September 30, 2029.
>
> • Failure to commence any phase of the Project within the specified deadline, as may be extended by applicable force majeure events, shall constitute a material default under the Agreement, subject to the default and cure provisions set forth in Section 15.
>
> • Developer shall provide quarterly construction progress reports to the City\'s Community Development Department, including percentage of completion, updated schedule, and identification of any delays or anticipated deviations from the approved timeline. Reports shall be submitted within 30 days following the end of each calendar quarter.

**[Section 14: Force Majeure]{.underline}**

> • **Definition:** \"Force majeure\" events shall include, without limitation: acts of God, fire, flood, earthquake, hurricane, tornado, severe weather events, pandemic, epidemic, quarantine, governmental health orders, war, terrorism, civil unrest, insurrection, strikes or labor disputes (other than those caused by Developer\'s labor practices or Developer\'s failure to comply with applicable labor laws), industry-wide supply chain disruptions beyond Developer\'s reasonable control, governmental moratoria on construction, and actions or inactions of governmental authorities (other than the City of Lakemont) that prevent or materially delay construction.
>
> • **Effect:** Force majeure events shall extend the applicable deadlines for commencement and Substantial Completion on a day-for-day basis for the duration of the force majeure event.
>
> • **Aggregate Cap:** The maximum extension of time available under this Section 14 shall not exceed **18 months in the aggregate** across the Project.
>
> • **Notice:** Developer shall provide written notice to the City within 15 business days of the occurrence of a force majeure event, describing in reasonable detail the nature of the event, its anticipated duration, and its expected impact on the Project timeline. Developer shall provide updated notices as circumstances change.
>
> • **Mitigation:** Developer shall use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects of any force majeure event, including but not limited to pursuing alternative suppliers, adjusting construction sequencing, and engaging additional contractors as reasonably available.
>
> • **Exclusions:** Force majeure shall not include: financial inability to perform, changes in market conditions, increases in the cost of labor or materials (unless attributable to an industry-wide supply chain disruption), Developer\'s failure to obtain financing, or any event caused by Developer\'s negligence or willful misconduct.

**[Section 15: Default and Cure]{.underline}**

> • **Monetary Defaults:** In the event of a monetary default by either party (including but not limited to Developer\'s failure to pay the land purchase price or the City\'s failure to fund committed infrastructure amounts), the non-defaulting party shall provide written notice specifying the nature and amount of the default. The defaulting party shall have **60 days** from receipt of such notice to cure the default.
>
> • **Non-Monetary Defaults:** In the event of a non-monetary default by either party (including but not limited to failure to commence construction within applicable deadlines, failure to comply with affordable housing requirements, or failure to maintain required insurance), the non-defaulting party shall provide written notice specifying the nature of the default in reasonable detail. The defaulting party shall have **90 days** from receipt of such notice to cure the default; provided, however, that if the nature of the default is such that it cannot reasonably be cured within 90 days, the defaulting party shall have such additional time as is reasonably necessary to effect a cure, so long as the defaulting party has commenced cure efforts within the initial 90-day period and is diligently and continuously pursuing cure to completion.
>
> • **Termination by City:** The City shall have the right to terminate the Agreement upon:
>
> \(a\) A material breach by Developer that remains uncured after the expiration of the applicable cure period;
>
> \(b\) Developer\'s failure to commence Phase I construction within 6 months following land conveyance;
>
> \(c\) Developer\'s abandonment of the Project, evidenced by cessation of all construction activity for a period of 180 consecutive days (other than cessation caused by force majeure events); and
>
> \(d\) Such other events of default as may be specified in the definitive Agreement.
>
> • **Termination by Developer:** Developer shall have the right to terminate the Agreement upon:
>
> \(a\) The City\'s failure to deliver clean, marketable, and insurable title to the Site at closing in accordance with Section 4;
>
> \(b\) The City\'s failure to fund its committed infrastructure obligations (\$12,500,000) within the timelines specified in the definitive Agreement;
>
> \(c\) A condemnation or taking by eminent domain (by any entity other than the City) of more than 15% of the Site; and
>
> \(d\) Such other events of default as may be specified in the definitive Agreement.
>
> • **Remedies:** Upon termination of the Agreement by a non-defaulting party, the non-defaulting party shall have all remedies available at law or in equity, including but not limited to damages, specific performance, and injunctive relief, subject in all cases to the dispute resolution provisions set forth in Section 17.

**[Section 16: Indemnification]{.underline}**

> • **Mutual Indemnification:** Each party shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the other party, and its officers, employees, agents, and representatives, from and against all claims, demands, actions, damages, losses, costs, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and court costs) arising from or related to the indemnifying party\'s negligent acts or omissions, reckless conduct, or willful misconduct in connection with the performance of its obligations under the Agreement.
>
> • **Developer Indemnification of City:** Developer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the City from and against all claims, demands, actions, damages, losses, costs, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising from or related to construction activities on the Site, including without limitation claims for personal injury, death, property damage, and workers\' compensation, regardless of whether such claims are brought by Developer\'s employees, contractors, subcontractors, or third parties.
>
> • **City Indemnification of Developer:** The City shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Developer, its members, officers, employees, agents, contractors, and successors from and against all claims, demands, actions, losses, costs, damages, liabilities, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising from or related to **pre-existing environmental conditions** on the City-owned parcels (Parcels 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017), including but not limited to third-party claims, governmental enforcement actions, and contribution or cost-recovery actions under federal or state environmental laws.
>
> • The indemnification obligations set forth in this Section 16 shall survive the expiration or termination of the Agreement.

**[Section 17: Dispute Resolution]{.underline}**

> • **Step 1 --- Negotiation:** The parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute arising under or related to the Agreement through good-faith negotiation between the City Manager (or designee) and the Developer\'s CEO (or designee) for a period of 30 days following written notice of the dispute by either party.
>
> • **Step 2 --- Mediation:** If the dispute is not resolved through negotiation within the 30-day period, the dispute shall be submitted to non-binding mediation before the **Lakemont Mediation Center**, or such other neutral mediator as the parties may agree upon. The costs of mediation, including mediator fees, shall be shared equally by the parties. Each party shall bear its own attorneys\' fees in connection with the mediation.
>
> • **Step 3 --- Binding Arbitration:** If the dispute is not resolved through mediation within 60 days following submission to the mediator, the dispute shall be submitted to final and binding arbitration administered under the **Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association (\"AAA\")**. The arbitration shall be conducted by a single arbitrator selected in accordance with the AAA rules. The arbitrator shall have experience in real estate development matters.
>
> • **Venue:** All mediation and arbitration proceedings shall be conducted in Cook County or Lake County, Illinois.
>
> • **Governing Law:** This Term Sheet and the definitive Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Illinois.
>
> • **Provisional Remedies:** Nothing in this Section 17 shall prevent either party from seeking temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, or other provisional judicial relief where necessary to prevent irreparable harm, pending resolution of the dispute through the procedures set forth above.

**[Section 18: Additional Terms]{.underline}**

**18.1 Representations and Warranties**

The definitive Agreement shall contain customary representations and warranties of each party, including without limitation:

> • Each party represents and warrants that it has full power and authority to enter into the Agreement and to perform its obligations thereunder, and that the execution and delivery of the Agreement has been duly authorized by all necessary action.
>
> • Developer represents and warrants that it has the financial capacity, development experience, and organizational resources necessary to complete the Project in accordance with the terms of the Agreement.
>
> • The City represents and warrants that it has good and marketable fee simple title to the Site, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, and adverse claims (except Permitted Exceptions), and that it has the legal authority to convey the Site to Developer.
>
> • The City represents and warrants that the Site is located within the Lakemont Central TIF District and that TIF funds are legally available for the purposes described in this Term Sheet, subject to annual appropriation and the availability of increment.

**18.2 Conditions Precedent to Execution of Definitive Agreement**

The execution of the definitive Agreement shall be subject to the satisfaction or waiver of the following conditions precedent:

> \(i\) Rezoning of the Site from M-2 to PD-TOD approved by the City Council and effective under applicable law;
>
> \(ii\) Parcel consolidation of Parcels 12-24-300-015, 12-24-300-016, and 12-24-300-017 completed or authorized by the applicable county authority;
>
> \(iii\) IEPA approval of the Remedial Action Plan for Parcel 12-24-300-016 under the Voluntary Site Remediation Program;
>
> \(iv\) Developer\'s demonstration of committed equity of at least \$85,000,000, in form and substance reasonably acceptable to the City;
>
> \(v\) Approval of the definitive Agreement by the Lakemont City Council; and
>
> \(vi\) Confirmation from First Prairie National Bank that the definitive Agreement is in a form acceptable to the lender as a condition of the construction loan commitment.

**18.3 Notices**

All notices, demands, requests, consents, approvals, and other communications required or permitted under the Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed delivered upon personal delivery, upon the date of delivery by nationally recognized overnight courier, or three business days following deposit in the United States mail, registered or certified, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, addressed as follows:

> **If to Developer:**
>
> Granite City Development LLC 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200 Chicago, IL 60607
>
> Attn: Marcus J. Pellegrini, Manager and CEO
>
> With a copy to:
>
> Thornfield & Pratt LLP 311 South Wacker Drive, Suite 5400 Chicago, IL 60606
>
> Attn: Catherine M. Albrecht
>
> **If to City:**
>
> City of Lakemont Office of the City Manager 200 Civic Center Drive Lakemont, IL 60048
>
> Attn: Gerald T. Fong, City Manager
>
> With a copy to:
>
> Office of Corporation Counsel City of Lakemont 200 Civic Center Drive Lakemont, IL 60048
>
> Attn: Rebecca A. Sinclair, Esq.
>
> With a copy to:
>
> Ashford, Keene & Mulvaney LLP 150 North Michigan Avenue, Suite 3200 Chicago, IL 60601
>
> Attn: Jonathan Ashford

Either party may change its notice address by providing written notice to the other party in accordance with this Section 18.3.

**18.4 Property Tax Abatement --- Supplemental Note**

As set forth in Section 5.4, the property tax abatement provided under this Term Sheet applies to Phase I improvements only. No property tax abatement is provided or contemplated for Phase II, Phase III, or Phase IV improvements under this Term Sheet. The parties\' respective positions regarding the financial structure of later phases, including any additional incentives, shall be addressed during negotiation of the definitive Agreement.

**[Section 19: Non-Binding Nature; Definitive Agreement]{.underline}**

This Term Sheet is non-binding and is for discussion purposes only. Neither party shall have any obligation to negotiate, execute, or deliver a definitive Agreement by reason of this Term Sheet, and either party may terminate discussions at any time for any reason or no reason.

The definitive Agreement shall contain customary representations, warranties, covenants, conditions precedent, default and cure provisions, remedies, exhibits (including a site plan, phasing plan, project budget, and form of restrictive covenant for affordable housing), and such other terms as the parties may negotiate in good faith. The definitive Agreement shall also include detailed provisions addressing, among other things, design review and approval procedures, construction monitoring, prevailing wage compliance, LEED certification, community benefit requirements, and ongoing reporting obligations.

The definitive Agreement is subject to approval by the Lakemont City Council at a duly noticed public meeting. The parties\' target date for execution of the definitive Agreement is June 2025, subject to the timely completion of rezoning, IEPA approvals, and satisfaction of other conditions precedent identified herein.

This Term Sheet shall be superseded in its entirety by the definitive Agreement upon execution and delivery thereof by both parties. Each party shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with the negotiation and preparation of this Term Sheet and the definitive Agreement, including legal fees, consultant fees, and other professional expenses.

**[Section 20: Confidentiality]{.underline}**

The terms and contents of this Term Sheet are confidential and shall not be disclosed by either party to any third party, except: (a) to each party\'s legal counsel, financial advisors, accountants, lenders, and equity investors, as reasonably necessary in connection with the evaluation and negotiation of the Project; (b) as required by applicable law or governmental regulation, including without limitation the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140/) as applicable to the City; and (c) to the extent necessary for City Council proceedings, committee meetings, and other governmental approvals related to the Project. Each party shall instruct its advisors and representatives to maintain the confidentiality of this Term Sheet in accordance with this Section 20.

**SIGNATURE PAGE**

This Term Sheet is executed by the authorized representatives of the parties as of the date first set forth above, for discussion purposes only, and does not constitute a binding agreement.
