**[Section 1: Executive Summary]{.underline}**

Granite City Development LLC (\"Developer\"), an Illinois limited liability company formed on March 14, 2019, is pleased to submit this proposal in response to the City of Lakemont\'s Request for Proposals No. 2024-CD-07 for the development of the approximately 12.7-acre site located at 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, Illinois 60048, adjacent to the new Lakemont Central Metra station. The Developer proposes to create **Lakemont Station Commons**, a transformative mixed-use transit-oriented development that will serve as a catalyst for economic growth, community vitality, and sustainable urban design in the City of Lakemont.

The site consists of three contiguous parcels --- PIN 12-24-300-015, PIN 12-24-300-016, and PIN 12-24-300-017 --- totaling approximately 12.7 acres (553,212 square feet), to be consolidated prior to development. Lakemont Station Commons will deliver approximately **1,620,000 gross square feet** of mixed-use development across four carefully sequenced phases, with an estimated total hard construction cost of **\$340,000,000** and a total project cost (inclusive of soft costs, land acquisition, financing costs, and environmental remediation) of approximately **\$412,000,000**.

The project program comprises:

> • **530 residential units**, including 106 affordable units priced at or below 60% of Area Median Income, representing 20% of all residential units;
>
> • **280,000 square feet** of Class A office space;
>
> • **63,000 square feet** of ground-floor retail;
>
> • A **150-room select-service hotel**;
>
> • A **2.5-acre public plaza** designed as the project\'s civic centerpiece;
>
> • A **28,000 square-foot community center** to be conveyed to the City upon completion; and
>
> • **1,430 structured parking spaces** across three parking facilities.

The four-phase delivery plan spans approximately eight to ten years, with Phase I groundbreaking targeted no later than **March 1, 2026**. The Developer is committed to achieving **LEED Gold certification** for all buildings within the development, reinforcing Lakemont Station Commons\' role as a model for sustainable transit-oriented design.

The project will deliver robust community benefits, including a local hiring commitment of 35% Lakemont residents for construction employment, affordable housing exceeding minimum requirements, and significant public amenities. The remediation of a former industrial brownfield site will eliminate legacy environmental contamination, converting a blighted property into a vibrant, productive community asset.

The Developer is led by **Marcus J. Pellegrini**, age 54, who brings 28 years of experience in Chicagoland commercial real estate development. Mr. Pellegrini has successfully developed notable projects including The Halsted Collection (mixed-use, Chicago), Riverside Crossing (transit-oriented development, Naperville), and Archer Park Residences (affordable housing, Chicago). The Developer entity maintains a net worth of approximately **\$185 million**, audited by Clearmont Accounting Group LLP, and has bonding capacity of up to \$75 million through Ridgepoint Surety Corporation.

The Developer\'s ownership comprises the Pellegrini Family Trust (60%), Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP (25%), and TransitWorks Equity Group LLC (15%). The project is supported by **\$85 million in committed equity** --- \$51 million from Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP and \$34 million from the Pellegrini Family Trust --- together with a **\$210 million preliminary construction loan commitment** from First Prairie National Bank. This capital base, combined with anticipated public incentives including TIF reimbursement of up to \$48 million, positions the project for successful execution across all four phases.

**[Section 2: Developer Qualifications and Experience]{.underline}**

**[2.1 Firm Overview]{.underline}**

Granite City Development LLC was formed on March 14, 2019, as an Illinois limited liability company, with its principal office located at 900 West Monroe Street, Suite 2200, Chicago, Illinois 60607. Since its formation, the Developer has focused exclusively on complex, large-scale mixed-use and transit-oriented development projects throughout the greater Chicagoland metropolitan area.

The firm is led by **Marcus J. Pellegrini**, who serves as Manager and Chief Executive Officer. Mr. Pellegrini, age 54, brings 28 years of hands-on experience in commercial real estate development, spanning project conceptualization, entitlement, financing, construction management, and lease-up. His career has encompassed over \$600 million in completed development projects across a range of product types, including mixed-use urban infill, transit-oriented residential, affordable housing, and commercial office developments.

The Developer entity maintains a net worth of approximately **\$185 million**, as reflected in its most recent audited financial statements prepared by Clearmont Accounting Group LLP. The Developer carries bonding capacity of up to **\$75 million** through Ridgepoint Surety Corporation, demonstrating the financial strength required to undertake a project of this scale and complexity. The firm has maintained clean litigation and regulatory compliance histories throughout its operating life.

**[2.2 Ownership Structure]{.underline}**

The ownership of Granite City Development LLC is composed of three members:

> **Pellegrini Family Trust --- 60% Membership Interest.** The Pellegrini Family Trust is the family trust of Marcus J. Pellegrini and serves as the majority owner and controlling member of the Developer. The Trust has committed **\$34 million** in equity to the Lakemont Station Commons project.
>
> **Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP --- 25% Membership Interest.** Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP is a Delaware limited partnership whose general partner is Lakeshore Capital Management LLC, led by Managing Partner Richard \"Rick\" Dominguez. Lakeshore Capital Partners maintains offices at 200 East Randolph Street, Suite 3100, Chicago, Illinois 60601. Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP has committed **\$51 million** in equity to the project, reflecting its deep conviction in the Lakemont Station Commons opportunity and its extensive experience co-investing in Chicagoland mixed-use developments.
>
> **TransitWorks Equity Group LLC --- 15% Membership Interest.** TransitWorks Equity Group LLC is a specialized real estate investment firm focused on transit-oriented development opportunities in major metropolitan areas. TransitWorks brings strategic expertise in TOD planning, public-private partnership structuring, and transit agency coordination.

Total committed equity from the Developer\'s ownership group is **\$85,000,000**, comprising the Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP contribution of \$51 million and the Pellegrini Family Trust contribution of \$34 million.

**[2.3 Relevant Project Experience]{.underline}**

The Developer and its principal, Mr. Pellegrini, have a proven track record of delivering complex, multi-phase, mixed-use developments in the Chicagoland area. The following projects are directly relevant to the proposed Lakemont Station Commons development:

**The Halsted Collection --- Chicago, Illinois.** The Halsted Collection is a 540,000 square-foot mixed-use development located in Chicago\'s Near West Side neighborhood, completed in 2021. The project comprises 280 residential units, 72,000 square feet of retail, and 35,000 square feet of community space, organized around a central courtyard with a public pedestrian connection to the adjacent CTA station. The development was executed as a public-private partnership with the City of Chicago, incorporating TIF financing, affordable housing set-asides, and prevailing wage compliance. The Halsted Collection demonstrated the Developer\'s ability to manage complex municipal approval processes, deliver mixed-income housing, and activate ground-floor retail in a transit-adjacent context. The project was completed on time and within budget, achieving stabilized occupancy within 14 months of initial move-in.

**Riverside Crossing --- Naperville, Illinois.** Riverside Crossing is a 380,000 square-foot transit-oriented development completed in 2023, located directly adjacent to the Naperville Metra station on the BNSF Railway line. The project includes 195 residential units, 120,000 square feet of Class A office space, and 22,000 square feet of ground-floor retail, supported by a 450-space structured parking facility. Riverside Crossing was developed in two phases over a four-year period, with Phase I (residential and parking) completed in 2021 and Phase II (office and retail) completed in 2023. This project is particularly relevant to Lakemont Station Commons because of its Metra station adjacency, phased construction approach, structured parking integration, and successful office leasing in a suburban TOD context. Riverside Crossing achieved LEED Silver certification, and Mr. Pellegrini personally managed the entitlement process, construction coordination with Metra, and structured financing involving both conventional construction debt and municipal incentives.

**Archer Park Residences --- Chicago, Illinois.** Archer Park Residences is a 160-unit affordable housing development located on Chicago\'s South Side, completed in 2020. The project provides housing for families earning at or below 60% of Area Median Income, with a mix of two-bedroom and three-bedroom units. Archer Park was developed in partnership with the Chicago Housing Authority and financed through a combination of Low-Income Housing Tax Credits, Tax-Exempt Bonds, and City of Chicago TIF funds. The project required extensive community engagement, compliance with HUD and IHDA affordability mandates, and long-term affordability covenant administration. Archer Park Residences demonstrates the Developer\'s capacity to deliver high-quality affordable housing within a public-private partnership framework --- experience directly applicable to the 106-unit affordable housing component of Lakemont Station Commons.

**[2.4 Development Team]{.underline}**

The Developer has assembled an experienced, multidisciplinary team to support the planning, entitlement, financing, and execution of Lakemont Station Commons:

> **Legal Counsel:** Thornfield & Pratt LLP --- Catherine M. Albrecht, Partner (lead); Daniel Hsu, Senior Associate. Thornfield & Pratt is a Chicago-based law firm with extensive experience in real estate development, public-private partnerships, TIF financing, and municipal law.
>
> **Environmental Consultant:** Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC --- completed the Phase II Environmental Site Assessment for the project site, report dated November 8, 2024. Greenfield will continue to serve as environmental advisor through remediation, IEPA VSRP enrollment, and NFR letter acquisition.
>
> **Appraisal:** Crossland Appraisal Services --- prepared the independent appraisal of the project site dated January 15, 2025, establishing an as-is value of \$18,400,000.
>
> **Auditing Firm:** Clearmont Accounting Group LLP --- independent auditors for Granite City Development LLC entity financial statements.
>
> **Bonding Company:** Ridgepoint Surety Corporation --- provider of performance and payment bonds for the project, with demonstrated capacity up to \$75 million.
>
> **Construction Lender (Preliminary):** First Prairie National Bank --- relationship manager Sandra L. Chen, 135 South LaSalle Street, Chicago, Illinois 60603. First Prairie has provided a preliminary commitment for a \$210 million construction loan facility, subject to execution of a development agreement in form satisfactory to the lender.
>
> **Architect, General Contractor, and Additional Consultants:** The Developer intends to retain a nationally recognized architectural firm experienced in transit-oriented and mixed-use design, as well as a top-tier general contractor with demonstrated capacity for projects of this scale. Specific firm selections will be finalized and submitted to the City upon selection of the Developer as the preferred development partner.

**[Section 3: Site Analysis and Environmental Conditions]{.underline}**

**[3.1 Site Description]{.underline}**

The proposed development site is located at 1400--1550 Lakemont Station Boulevard, Lakemont, Illinois 60048. The site comprises three contiguous parcels:

> • **Parcel 12-24-300-015** --- approximately 3.8 acres
>
> • **Parcel 12-24-300-016** --- approximately 4.1 acres
>
> • **Parcel 12-24-300-017** --- approximately 4.8 acres

The combined site area totals approximately **12.7 acres (553,212 square feet)**. All three parcels are currently owned by the City of Lakemont, having been acquired through a combination of tax forfeiture proceedings in 2016 (Parcels 015 and 016) and direct purchase in 2018 (Parcel 017). The site was formerly occupied by the Lakemont Ironworks manufacturing campus, which ceased operations in 2009. Since closure, the property has remained vacant and underutilized.

The site is currently zoned **M-2 (General Manufacturing)**, which is incompatible with the proposed mixed-use residential, commercial, and hotel program. Rezoning to **PD-TOD (Planned Development--Transit Oriented District)** will be required, as discussed in Section 3.3 below.

The site\'s most significant asset is its direct adjacency to the new **Lakemont Central Metra station**, which provides commuter rail service on the Milwaukee District North Line to downtown Chicago. The station is anticipated to serve approximately 1,200 daily riders at initial service levels, with projected ridership growth as surrounding development increases density and transit demand. The station\'s pedestrian entrance is located within approximately 400 feet of the eastern boundary of the project site, providing exceptional transit connectivity that is foundational to the project\'s transit-oriented design concept.

An independent appraisal conducted by **Crossland Appraisal Services**, dated January 15, 2025, established an as-is fair market value of **\$18,400,000** for the combined three-parcel site. Pursuant to the City\'s incentive policy for transit-oriented development on City-owned land, the Developer proposes a purchase price of **\$9,200,000**, representing 50% of the appraised value. The resulting land write-down of \$9,200,000 reflects the City\'s investment in catalyzing the redevelopment of this brownfield site.

Prior to development, parcel consolidation will be required to merge the three existing tax parcels into a single development parcel or into phase-specific sub-parcels, as appropriate. The Developer will coordinate with the Lake County Recorder\'s Office and the City of Lakemont to effectuate the necessary consolidation.

**[3.2 Environmental Conditions and Remediation Approach]{.underline}**

A comprehensive Phase II Environmental Site Assessment was completed by **Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC**, with a report dated November 8, 2024. The Phase II ESA included soil borings, groundwater sampling, and laboratory analysis across all three parcels. The findings are summarized as follows:

**Parcel 12-24-300-015:** No contamination was detected above applicable IEPA Tiered Approach to Corrective Action Objectives (TACO) residential standards. No further action is required for this parcel, and it is suitable for immediate development without remediation.

**Parcel 12-24-300-016:** The Phase II ESA identified petroleum hydrocarbon contamination, specifically BTEX compounds (benzene, toluene, ethylbenzene, and xylene), in both soil and shallow groundwater across approximately 1.8 acres of the 4.1-acre parcel. The contamination is consistent with historical fuel storage and industrial operations associated with the former Lakemont Ironworks. Remediation is required to achieve residential-use cleanup standards under the IEPA\'s Tiered Approach to Corrective Action Objectives. The Developer proposes enrollment in the **IEPA Voluntary Site Remediation Program (VSRP)** and preparation of a Remedial Action Plan (RAP) for IEPA review and approval. The estimated remediation timeline is **14 to 18 months**, and the estimated remediation cost is **\$2,800,000**. A **No Further Remediation (NFR) letter** must be obtained from IEPA prior to residential development on this parcel.

**Parcel 12-24-300-017:** The Phase II ESA identified low-level metals contamination in shallow soil, consistent with urban fill material commonly found on former industrial sites. Contaminant concentrations are below TACO residential standards for most parameters but warrant the installation of an engineered barrier (clean fill cap) as a precautionary measure to ensure long-term suitability for residential use. The estimated cost for the engineered barrier is **\$340,000**.

Total estimated environmental remediation costs for the project site are **\$3,140,000** (\$2,800,000 for Parcel 016 plus \$340,000 for Parcel 017). The Developer intends to commence remediation activities concurrent with pre-construction site preparation, targeting completion of Parcel 016 remediation and receipt of the NFR letter before vertical construction on that parcel begins. Environmental remediation costs are listed as eligible expenditures under the Lakemont Central TIF District, and the Developer anticipates that these costs will be offset through TIF reimbursement as increment becomes available.

The Developer\'s remediation approach will prioritize coordination with IEPA to minimize schedule risk. Greenfield Environmental Consultants LLC will continue to serve as the project\'s environmental consultant through remediation completion and NFR letter acquisition, ensuring continuity of the environmental assessment and remediation team throughout the regulatory process.

**[3.3 Rezoning Strategy]{.underline}**

The site\'s current **M-2 (General Manufacturing)** zoning classification is incompatible with the proposed mixed-use program of residential, office, retail, hotel, and public amenity uses. The Developer will work collaboratively with the City of Lakemont Community Development Department, under the direction of Director Paul R. Vasquez, to pursue rezoning of the entire site to **PD-TOD (Planned Development--Transit Oriented District)**.

The PD-TOD zoning classification is anticipated to provide the regulatory framework necessary to accommodate the density, building heights, use mix, and design standards contemplated by the Lakemont Station Commons plan. The Developer understands that rezoning must be completed and approved by the Lakemont City Council prior to execution of the development agreement. Based on consultation with City staff and review of the City\'s zoning ordinance and procedural requirements, the Developer anticipates that the rezoning process --- including application filing, plan commission review, public hearings, and City Council action --- will require approximately four to six months from the date of application filing through final City Council approval.

**[Section 4: Project Description --- Lakemont Station Commons]{.underline}**

**[4.1 Design Vision and Principles]{.underline}**

Lakemont Station Commons is conceived as a comprehensively planned, transit-oriented, mixed-use community that maximizes the extraordinary locational advantage of direct adjacency to the Lakemont Central Metra station. The project\'s design vision is rooted in several foundational principles that together will create a vibrant, sustainable, and enduring contribution to the City of Lakemont.

**Transit-Oriented Connectivity.** The project\'s site planning, building orientation, and pedestrian circulation systems are designed to prioritize and celebrate the connection to the Metra station. Primary pedestrian routes will provide direct, weather-protected paths from the station entrance through the project\'s central public plaza and into the residential, office, retail, and hotel components. Bicycle storage and repair facilities will be distributed throughout the development, with a minimum of one bicycle storage space per two residential units and dedicated bicycle parking at each commercial building entrance. Electric vehicle charging stations will be provided at a minimum of 10% of all parking spaces.

**Walkable, Mixed-Use Environment.** The ground-floor program is designed to create an activated, pedestrian-friendly streetscape along Lakemont Station Boulevard. A continuous retail frontage of 63,000 square feet will provide neighborhood-serving shops, restaurants, and services that enliven the street and generate foot traffic throughout the day and evening. Upper floors will accommodate residential units, office suites, and hotel rooms, creating the density and diversity of uses that sustain a vibrant urban environment.

**Civic Centerpiece.** The 2.5-acre public plaza is the project\'s defining public space --- a central gathering area for the entire Lakemont community, designed to host seasonal events, farmers\' markets, concerts, and daily community life. The plaza will incorporate high-quality landscaping, public seating areas, water features, and commissioned public art installations. Adjacent to the plaza, the 28,000 square-foot community center will serve as a civic anchor, providing multi-purpose meeting rooms, potential library space, and a community kitchen.

**Sustainability and LEED Gold Certification.** The Developer is committed to achieving **LEED Gold certification** for all buildings within Lakemont Station Commons. This commitment reflects the project\'s aspiration to set the standard for sustainable development in the Lakemont region. To achieve LEED Gold, the Developer will implement a comprehensive suite of sustainable design strategies, including high-performance building envelopes with above-code insulation values, energy-efficient HVAC systems with heat recovery, LED lighting with daylight harvesting controls, low-flow plumbing fixtures, use of recycled and regionally sourced building materials, green roofs on the residential towers, and advanced stormwater management systems including bioswales, permeable paving, and rain gardens integrated into the public plaza design. Each building will be registered with the U.S. Green Building Council (USGBC) during the design phase, and the Developer will pursue formal LEED Gold certification upon completion of each building. The development team will engage a LEED-accredited professional to guide the design and documentation process from schematic design through construction and commissioning.

**Brownfield Transformation.** Lakemont Station Commons will transform a former industrial brownfield --- vacant since 2009 --- into a productive, tax-generating, community-serving asset. The remediation of petroleum hydrocarbons and metals contamination will eliminate an environmental liability and restore the site to its highest and best use.

Affordable units will be restricted to households earning at or below 60% of Area Median Income and will include a mix of studio, one-bedroom, and two-bedroom floor plans distributed throughout the residential buildings. The 63,000 square feet of retail will be delivered across Phases I and II, establishing a complete retail environment early in the project\'s lifecycle. The 150-room hotel in Phase III and the public amenities in Phase IV complete the mixed-use program.

**[Section 5: Phasing Plan and Construction Timeline]{.underline}**

**[5.1 Phasing Overview]{.underline}**

Lakemont Station Commons will be delivered in four carefully sequenced phases designed to manage construction risk, align with market absorption rates, optimize financing capacity, and ensure that each phase builds on the infrastructure, market momentum, and community identity established by its predecessor. The overall project timeline spans approximately **eight to ten years** from Phase I groundbreaking (March 2026) through Phase IV completion (approximately 2034 under the maximum contractual timeline).

The phasing sequence is as follows:

> **Phase I** (Months 0--30): Residential Tower A, retail podium, and parking garage. March 2026 through September 2028.
>
> **Phase II** (Commences within 12 months of Phase I Substantial Completion): Office building, additional retail, and parking garage. Estimated commencement by September 2029; duration 24 months; estimated completion by approximately September 2031.
>
> **Phase III** (Commences within 12 months of Phase II Substantial Completion): Residential Tower B and select-service hotel. Estimated commencement by September 2032; duration 30 months; estimated completion by approximately March 2035.
>
> **Phase IV** (Commences concurrently with Phase III): Public plaza, community center, and public parking structure. Estimated commencement by September 2032; duration 24 months; estimated completion by approximately September 2034.

The Developer maintains a **target accelerated schedule** that is approximately two years faster than the maximum contractual deadlines described above. Under the accelerated scenario, Phase II commencement would occur in late 2028, Phase III/IV commencement in late 2030, and full project buildout by approximately 2033. The contractual maximum deadlines, however, govern for development agreement purposes and provide adequate buffer for regulatory, market, and construction contingencies.

The phasing plan is designed so that Phase I serves as the project catalyst, establishing the residential community and activating ground-floor retail to create initial market proof of concept and generate the first TIF increment. Phase II leverages the residential base to attract office tenants. Phase III adds residential density and a hospitality component. Phase IV delivers the public amenities --- plaza, community center, and public parking --- that complete the community vision. The Developer anticipates that TIF reimbursement of up to \$48,000,000 will flow over the life of the TIF district as increment is generated by each completed phase, supporting the overall project financing structure.

**[5.2 Phase I --- Residential Tower A and Retail Podium]{.underline}**

**Program:** Phase I comprises 320 residential units (256 market-rate and 64 affordable at or below 60% AMI), 45,000 square feet of ground-floor retail, and a 480-space structured parking garage. Total gross building area: 420,000 square feet. Estimated hard construction cost: **\$105,000,000**.

**Timeline:** Groundbreaking will occur no later than **March 1, 2026**. The construction duration for Phase I is 30 months, with Substantial Completion targeted for **September 30, 2028**.

**Pre-Construction Activities.** Prior to groundbreaking, several critical pre-construction milestones must be achieved. Environmental remediation of Parcel 016 will commence following enrollment in the IEPA Voluntary Site Remediation Program, with an estimated remediation timeline of 14 to 18 months and a target of obtaining the No Further Remediation letter before vertical construction begins on that parcel. Parcel consolidation, rezoning to PD-TOD, site preparation and grading, and utility connections will be completed during the pre-construction period.

**Infrastructure.** The Developer will be responsible for **\$8,300,000** in on-site infrastructure improvements, including internal roadways, sidewalks, site grading, stormwater management, utility connections, and site lighting. The City has committed **\$12,500,000** toward off-site infrastructure improvements essential to Phase I, including road widening of Lakemont Station Boulevard, installation of a new traffic signal at the Oak Street intersection, water main extension, and sanitary sewer upgrade to accommodate the project\'s density. Coordination between the Developer\'s on-site construction and the City\'s off-site improvements will be critical to the Phase I schedule.

**Affordable Housing.** Phase I delivers 64 affordable units --- 20% of the 320 total residential units --- restricted to households earning at or below 60% of Area Median Income. Affordable units will be integrated throughout the building and will include a mix of unit types comparable to the market-rate units.

**Sustainability.** Phase I is designed and will be constructed to LEED Gold standards, with LEED certification to be pursued upon completion.

**Performance Security.** The Developer will provide a **\$15 million performance bond or irrevocable letter of credit** prior to land conveyance, as required by the City, to be issued by Ridgepoint Surety Corporation.

**Property Tax Abatement.** Phase I will benefit from a **12-year declining property tax abatement**: Years 1--4 at 75%; Years 5--8 at 50%; Years 9--12 at 25%; Year 13 and beyond at 0% (full tax obligation). This abatement is a critical incentive supporting the Phase I pro forma and enabling the affordable housing component.

Phase I is the foundational element of Lakemont Station Commons. Its successful delivery will establish the development\'s market identity, generate initial TIF increment for reimbursement of eligible costs, and demonstrate the viability of the transit-oriented concept for subsequent phases.

**[5.3 Phase II --- Office Building and Retail]{.underline}**

**Program:** Phase II comprises 280,000 square feet of Class A office space, 18,000 square feet of additional retail, and a 350-space structured parking garage. Total gross building area: 380,000 square feet. Estimated hard construction cost: **\$76,000,000**.

**Timeline:** Phase II will commence within **12 months** of Phase I Substantial Completion. Construction duration is 24 months. Assuming Phase I achieves Substantial Completion on schedule in September 2028, Phase II commencement would occur by September 2029, with Substantial Completion by approximately September 2031.

Phase II is strategically positioned to capitalize on the residential community and retail activation established by Phase I. The Class A office component will attract corporate and professional tenants seeking transit-accessible suburban office space, leveraging the Lakemont Central Metra station\'s connectivity to downtown Chicago. The additional 18,000 square feet of retail in Phase II brings the project\'s total retail footprint to 63,000 square feet, creating a critical mass of neighborhood-serving retail that enhances the project\'s appeal to both residents and office tenants.

**Sustainability.** Phase II will be designed and constructed to LEED Gold standards, with certification to be pursued upon completion.

**Permit Fee Waiver.** The City will waive building permit fees for Phase II, with estimated savings of **\$480,000**. Combined with the Phase I permit fee waiver of \$920,000, total permit fee savings for the project\'s first two phases are \$1,400,000.

**Property Tax Abatement.** No property tax abatement applies to Phase II. The 12-year declining abatement is limited to Phase I. Phase II improvements will bear the full property tax obligation from the date of completion.

The Phase II office component is expected to be a significant driver of incremental property tax revenue and TIF increment, contributing to the reimbursement of eligible project costs over the remaining life of the TIF district.

**[5.4 Phase III --- Residential Tower B and Hotel]{.underline}**

**Program:** Phase III comprises 210 residential units (168 market-rate and 42 affordable at or below 60% AMI) and a 150-room select-service hotel. Total gross building area: 440,000 square feet. Estimated hard construction cost: **\$88,000,000**.

**Timeline:** Phase III will commence within **12 months** of Phase II Substantial Completion. Construction duration is 30 months. Assuming Phase II achieves Substantial Completion in approximately September 2031, Phase III commencement would occur by September 2032, with Substantial Completion by approximately March 2035 under the maximum contractual timeline.

The hotel brand and flag will be selected by the Developer based on prevailing market conditions at the time of Phase III execution. The Developer anticipates a select-service format that complements the residential and office uses and serves both business travelers and visitors to the Lakemont community.

**Affordable Housing.** Phase III delivers 42 affordable units --- 20% of the 210 total residential units --- restricted to households earning at or below 60% of AMI. Combined with the 64 affordable units in Phase I, the project-wide total is 106 affordable units out of 530 total residential units, maintaining the overall 20% affordability commitment.

**Sustainability.** Both the residential tower and the hotel will be designed and constructed to LEED Gold standards, with certification to be pursued upon completion of each building.

**Property Tax Abatement.** No property tax abatement applies to Phase III. Phase III improvements will bear the full property tax obligation from the date of completion.

Phase III represents the final phase of income-producing development, adding significant residential density and the hospitality component that completes the project\'s mixed-use program. The assessed value of the Phase III improvements will contribute to TIF increment generation during the remaining years of the TIF district.

**[5.5 Phase IV --- Public Plaza, Community Center, and Parking Structure]{.underline}**

**Program:** Phase IV comprises the **2.5-acre public plaza** with landscaping and public art installations, the **28,000 square-foot community center** (to be designed to City specifications and conveyed to the City upon completion), and a **600-space public parking structure** serving Metra commuters, retail visitors, and project residents and workers. Total gross building area: 380,000 square feet. Estimated hard construction cost: **\$71,000,000**.

**Timeline:** Phase IV will commence **concurrently with Phase III** --- that is, within 12 months of Phase II Substantial Completion. Construction duration is 24 months. Estimated commencement is approximately September 2032, with Substantial Completion by approximately September 2034.

**Community Center.** The community center is a signature public benefit of Lakemont Station Commons. It will be designed to accommodate multi-purpose programming, including community meeting rooms, flexible event space, a potential library branch, and a community kitchen. The Developer will work closely with the City to finalize the program, design specifications, and operational requirements prior to construction. Upon completion, the community center will be conveyed to the City of Lakemont in fee simple, fully furnished and equipped per the agreed-upon specifications.

**Public Plaza.** The 2.5-acre public plaza is designed as the civic heart of Lakemont Station Commons --- a central gathering space that connects the project\'s residential, office, retail, and hotel components and provides a welcoming public amenity for the broader Lakemont community. The plaza design incorporates high-quality hardscape and softscape elements, seating areas, water features, seasonal planting beds, and commissioned public art installations. The plaza will be designed to accommodate seasonal programming, including farmers\' markets, outdoor concerts, holiday events, and community festivals.

**Public Parking Structure.** The 600-space parking structure will serve the dual purpose of accommodating Metra commuter parking and providing publicly accessible parking for the retail, restaurant, community center, and plaza uses. The parking structure is classified as public infrastructure for TIF eligibility purposes.

**Sustainability.** The community center will be designed and constructed to LEED Gold standards, with certification to be pursued upon completion. The parking structure and public plaza will be designed to LEED for Neighborhood Development standards, incorporating green infrastructure, energy-efficient lighting, and EV charging capacity.

**Property Tax Abatement.** No property tax abatement applies to Phase IV. Phase IV improvements, to the extent they are privately assessed, will bear the full property tax obligation from the date of completion.

Phase IV is the culmination of the Lakemont Station Commons vision, delivering the public amenities and community spaces that are central to the City\'s goals for the Lakemont Central Metra station area. Phase IV costs are partially TIF-eligible, including the parking structure as public infrastructure, any remaining environmental remediation costs, and qualified public improvement expenditures.

**[5.6 Phasing Interdependencies and Schedule Contingencies]{.underline}**

The phasing plan for Lakemont Station Commons is structured as a sequential delivery program in which each phase\'s commencement is triggered by the prior phase\'s achievement of Substantial Completion, with the exception of Phase IV, which commences concurrently with Phase III. This sequential structure ensures that infrastructure is in place, market conditions are validated, and financing is secured before subsequent phases are initiated.

The Developer recognizes that a multi-phase project of this scale and duration is subject to a range of potential delay factors, including adverse weather conditions, supply chain disruptions, labor availability, regulatory approval timelines, and changes in market conditions. The Developer has incorporated adequate buffer within each phase\'s construction duration to accommodate typical construction delays without triggering milestone failures.

For extraordinary events beyond the Developer\'s reasonable control, the Developer anticipates standard force majeure protections providing day-for-day extensions for qualifying events. The Developer expects that an aggregate force majeure extension cap of **18 months** across the full project life will be sufficient to address unforeseeable delays while maintaining the overall project timeline integrity. The 18-month aggregate cap, combined with the buffer built into each phase\'s construction duration and the Developer\'s target accelerated schedule (which is approximately two years ahead of contractual maximums), provides substantial schedule resilience.

The Developer\'s target accelerated schedule is the Developer\'s business plan objective, designed to maximize market timing, accelerate TIF increment generation, and deliver community benefits as early as practicable. The contractual maximum deadlines serve as the outer boundary, providing the flexibility necessary to navigate the inherent uncertainties of a decade-long, multi-phase development program.

**[Section 6: Community Benefits and Social Impact]{.underline}**

**[6.1 Affordable Housing Commitment]{.underline}**

The Developer is committed to delivering **106 affordable housing units** across Lakemont Station Commons, representing **20%** of the project\'s 530 total residential units. Affordable units will be restricted to households earning at or below 60% of Area Median Income, as determined by HUD income limits for the Chicago-Naperville-Elgin Metropolitan Statistical Area.

The affordable units will be distributed across two phases:

> • **Phase I:** 64 affordable units out of 320 total residential units (20%)
>
> • **Phase III:** 42 affordable units out of 210 total residential units (20%)

The affordable housing program is designed to ensure integration rather than segregation. Affordable units will be distributed throughout each residential building --- not concentrated on lower floors, separate wings, or less desirable locations. Affordable unit types will include a mix of studio, one-bedroom, and two-bedroom floor plans, with interior finishes and amenity access comparable to market-rate units. Residents of affordable units will have full access to all building amenities, including fitness centers, rooftop terraces, bicycle storage, and common areas.

Affordability will be maintained for a **minimum of 30 years** from the date of initial occupancy of each phase, secured through a recorded affordability covenant or regulatory agreement. The Developer will work with the City to establish monitoring, compliance, and reporting procedures to ensure long-term adherence to the affordability requirements.

The Developer\'s commitment to affordable housing is informed by its successful delivery of the Archer Park Residences in Chicago, a 160-unit affordable development completed in compliance with HUD and Illinois Housing Development Authority requirements. The Developer\'s team includes experienced affordable housing professionals who understand the regulatory, operational, and financial complexities of delivering and maintaining income-restricted housing within a mixed-income community.

**[6.2 Local Hiring and Workforce Development]{.underline}**

The Developer is committed to maximizing the economic benefit of Lakemont Station Commons for Lakemont residents. The Developer commits to a goal of **35% Lakemont residents** for construction employment across all four phases of the project. This commitment encompasses both skilled trade positions and general construction labor.

To achieve this goal, the Developer will implement a **first-source hiring program** in partnership with the **Lakemont Workforce Center**, whereby the Workforce Center will serve as the initial referral source for both construction and permanent positions generated by the project. The Developer and its general contractor will notify the Lakemont Workforce Center of available positions prior to broader recruitment, giving Lakemont residents priority access to employment opportunities.

The Developer will conduct outreach to local trade unions and community organizations to identify qualified Lakemont residents for skilled trade positions. In addition, the Developer will work with the Lakemont Workforce Center to develop **pre-apprenticeship and apprenticeship programs** that create pathways for Lakemont residents to enter the construction trades, with a focus on populations underrepresented in the skilled trades workforce.

The Developer will provide **quarterly reporting** to the City on local hiring metrics, including the number and percentage of Lakemont residents employed, hours worked, wages paid, and trade categories represented. These reports will be submitted to the Community Development Department for review and will be made available to the City Council upon request.

**[6.3 Public Amenities and Community Spaces]{.underline}**

Lakemont Station Commons will deliver significant public amenities that serve the broader Lakemont community:

**2.5-Acre Public Plaza.** The central public plaza is designed as a year-round community gathering space, featuring high-quality landscaping, public seating, water features, and commissioned public art. The plaza will be programmed for seasonal events, including farmers\' markets, outdoor concerts, holiday festivals, and community celebrations. The Developer will work with the City to establish a programming plan that activates the plaza throughout the year.

**28,000 Square-Foot Community Center.** The community center will be a purpose-built civic facility incorporating multi-purpose rooms, flexible meeting and event space, a potential library branch (subject to coordination with the Lakemont Public Library District), and a community kitchen. The facility will be conveyed to the City upon completion and will be designed to accommodate a wide range of community programming.

**600-Space Public Parking Structure.** The public parking facility will serve Metra commuters, retail and restaurant patrons, community center and plaza visitors, and project residents and workers. Publicly accessible parking is a critical element of the project\'s transit-oriented function, supporting ridership growth at the Lakemont Central Metra station.

**Activated Streetscape.** The 63,000 square feet of ground-floor retail along Lakemont Station Boulevard will create a pedestrian-friendly, commercially vibrant streetscape that contributes to the public realm and provides neighborhood-serving goods and services.

**[6.4 Environmental Sustainability]{.underline}**

Sustainability is a core principle of Lakemont Station Commons. The Developer is committed to achieving **LEED Gold certification or equivalent** for all buildings within the development, ensuring that the project meets the highest standards of environmental performance and resource efficiency.

The Developer\'s sustainability strategy encompasses the following elements:

> • **Energy Efficiency:** High-performance building envelopes exceeding code-minimum insulation requirements; energy-efficient HVAC systems with heat recovery ventilation; LED lighting with daylight harvesting and occupancy sensor controls; ENERGY STAR-rated appliances in all residential units.
>
> • **Water Conservation:** Low-flow plumbing fixtures throughout all buildings; drought-tolerant landscaping in the public plaza and common areas; rainwater harvesting systems for landscape irrigation.
>
> • **Materials:** Use of recycled-content building materials; specification of regionally sourced materials to reduce transportation-related carbon emissions; construction waste management plans targeting a minimum 75% diversion rate.
>
> • **Green Infrastructure:** Green roofs on both residential towers, providing thermal insulation, stormwater retention, and urban habitat; bioswales, permeable paving, and rain gardens integrated into the public plaza and site landscaping for on-site stormwater management.
>
> • **Transportation:** EV charging stations at a minimum of 10% of all parking spaces; bicycle storage at a minimum of one space per two residential units; dedicated bicycle parking at all commercial building entrances; proximity to Metra commuter rail reducing private vehicle dependency and vehicle miles traveled.
>
> • **Brownfield Remediation:** The conversion of a contaminated former industrial site to a productive, healthy community represents a fundamental sustainability achievement --- eliminating environmental contamination, preventing continued site degradation, and restoring the land to its highest and best use.

The Developer will register each building with the U.S. Green Building Council during the design phase and will pursue formal LEED Gold certification for each building upon completion. A LEED-accredited professional will be retained to guide the integrated design process, manage LEED documentation, and oversee the certification submission process through the USGBC review.

  Permit Fee Waivers                                     \$1,400,000

  Additional Debt/Mezzanine (Phases II--IV)              \$58,400,000

  **Total Sources**                                      **\$412,000,000**
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[7.3 Public Incentives Summary]{.underline}**

The Developer requests or anticipates the following public incentives from the City of Lakemont in support of the Lakemont Station Commons project:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Incentive**                                               **Estimated Value**
  ----------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Land Write-Down (50% of appraised value)                    \$9,200,000

  TIF Reimbursement (eligible costs, pay-as-you-go, capped)   Up to \$48,000,000

  City Off-Site Infrastructure Investment                     \$12,500,000

  Property Tax Abatement (Phase I, 12-year declining)         Estimated NPV significant

  Building Permit Fee Waivers (Phases I & II)                 \$1,400,000
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The City\'s total direct capital commitment (infrastructure, land write-down, and permit fee waivers) exceeds **\$23 million**, supplemented by TIF reimbursement of up to \$48 million funded by the project\'s own tax increment.

The return on this public investment is substantial. Lakemont Station Commons will generate an expanded and diversified tax base upon TIF district expiration, create an estimated 1,800 construction jobs and 900 permanent jobs, deliver 106 units of affordable housing, remediate a brownfield site, and provide lasting public amenities including a community center, public plaza, and public parking facility. The project will transform a vacant, contaminated former industrial campus into one of the most significant transit-oriented developments in the Chicago metropolitan region.

**Infrastructure Cost-Sharing.** The total infrastructure budget for the project is **\$20,800,000**, comprising \$12,500,000 in City-funded off-site improvements and \$8,300,000 in Developer-funded on-site improvements. The City\'s off-site investment includes road widening of Lakemont Station Boulevard, a new traffic signal at the Oak Street intersection, water main extension, and sanitary sewer upgrade. The Developer\'s on-site investment includes internal roadways, sidewalks, stormwater management, utility connections, and site lighting. This cost-sharing model reflects the shared public-private nature of the investment and ensures that both on-site and off-site infrastructure are constructed to the standards necessary to support the project\'s density and the broader community.

**[7.4 Tax Increment Financing Analysis]{.underline}**

The project site is located within the **Lakemont Central TIF District**, established by Ordinance No. 2019-42, with a statutory expiration date of **December 31, 2042**. The TIF District encompasses the project site and surrounding parcels, and was established to facilitate the redevelopment of the former Lakemont Ironworks campus and adjacent underutilized properties.

The Developer anticipates TIF reimbursement of up to **\$48,000,000** for eligible costs, which include public infrastructure improvements, environmental remediation expenditures, parking structure construction, and other qualifying expenditures under the TIF Act (65 ILCS 5/11-74.4-1 et seq.) and the TIF District\'s Redevelopment Plan. TIF reimbursement will be administered on a **pay-as-you-go basis**: the Developer will fund eligible expenditures upfront and submit annual reimbursement requests from the tax increment generated by the project\'s increased assessed value above the frozen base.

The Developer\'s incremental TIF projection is as follows:

> **Phase I Completion (approximately 2028):** Upon completion and assessment of the Phase I improvements (320 residential units, 45,000 sq ft retail, 480-space parking), partial tax increment will begin to flow to the TIF fund. The Developer estimates initial annual increment in the range of **\$1,500,000 to \$2,000,000** per year, reflecting the Phase I assessed value and the Phase I property tax abatement (which reduces but does not eliminate the TIF-eligible increment).
>
> **Phase II Completion (approximately 2031):** The addition of 280,000 sq ft of Class A office, 18,000 sq ft of retail, and 350-space parking will significantly increase the project\'s assessed value and annual increment. The Developer estimates annual increment will grow to approximately **\$2,800,000 to \$3,200,000** per year upon Phase II stabilization.
>
> **Phase III and Phase IV Completion (approximately 2034--2035):** Full buildout of all project components --- including Residential Tower B, the hotel, the community center, public plaza, and public parking structure --- will bring the annual TIF increment to an estimated **\$4,200,000 per year** at full assessment.
>
> **Cumulative Increment through TIF Expiration (December 31, 2042):** Based on the above projections, the Developer estimates that total cumulative TIF reimbursement over the life of the district will be in the range of **\$40,000,000 to \$48,000,000**, which approaches or reaches the \$48 million cap.

This projection assumes that phases are completed on or ahead of the target accelerated schedule, assessment appeals are resolved in a timely manner, and equalized assessed values reflect the anticipated improvements. The Developer will work with the City to monitor annual increment generation and coordinate reimbursement requests to maximize the project\'s TIF benefit within the district\'s remaining life.

**[Section 8: Infrastructure Plan]{.underline}**

**[8.1 On-Site Infrastructure]{.underline}**

The Developer will be responsible for the design and construction of **\$8,300,000** in on-site infrastructure improvements necessary to support the Lakemont Station Commons development. On-site infrastructure will be constructed primarily during Phase I, with supplemental connections and extensions for subsequent phases as each phase commences.

On-site infrastructure improvements include:

> • Internal roadway network providing vehicular circulation throughout the development, including service and emergency access routes
>
> • Sidewalks, pedestrian pathways, and bicycle routes connecting all project buildings to the public plaza, Metra station, and Lakemont Station Boulevard
>
> • Site grading and earthwork to establish finished grades for all building pads, roadways, and the public plaza
>
> • Stormwater management systems, including on-site detention, bioswales, permeable paving areas, and rain gardens, designed to meet or exceed local and IEPA stormwater management requirements
>
> • Utility connections for water service, sanitary sewer, electrical power, natural gas, and telecommunications, extending from the public right-of-way or utility mains to each building location
>
> • Site lighting for internal roadways, pedestrian paths, parking areas, and the public plaza, utilizing energy-efficient LED fixtures with dark-sky-compliant design
>
> • Landscaping within the development parcels, including street trees, ornamental plantings, and green infrastructure elements consistent with the LEED Gold design standards

The Developer will coordinate on-site infrastructure construction with the City\'s off-site improvement schedule to ensure seamless integration of utility connections, roadway alignments, and stormwater management systems.

**[8.2 Off-Site Infrastructure (City-Funded)]{.underline}**

The City of Lakemont has committed **\$12,500,000** toward off-site infrastructure improvements that are critical prerequisites for the Lakemont Station Commons development. These improvements will be designed and constructed by or on behalf of the City, with the Developer providing coordination and input to ensure compatibility with the on-site development program.

Off-site infrastructure improvements include:

> • **Road Widening of Lakemont Station Boulevard:** Widening of the existing two-lane roadway to a four-lane cross-section with a center turn lane, curb-and-gutter improvements, and dedicated bicycle lanes, extending from the project site\'s eastern boundary to the intersection with Main Street. This improvement is essential to accommodate the traffic generated by the project\'s 1,620,000 square feet of development and 1,430 parking spaces.
>
> • **New Traffic Signal at Oak Street Intersection:** Installation of a fully actuated traffic signal at the intersection of Lakemont Station Boulevard and Oak Street, located at the project\'s primary vehicular entrance. The traffic signal is required to manage turning movements and ensure safe pedestrian crossings between the project site and the Metra station.
>
> • **Water Main Extension:** Extension of a 16-inch water main from the existing trunk line on Main Street along Lakemont Station Boulevard to serve the project site, providing adequate domestic water supply and fire flow capacity for the full buildout.
>
> • **Sanitary Sewer Upgrade:** Upgrade of the existing 12-inch sanitary sewer line along Lakemont Station Boulevard to a 24-inch line, with a new connection to the regional interceptor, to accommodate the wastewater flows generated by the project\'s residential, commercial, and hotel uses.

The combined on-site and off-site infrastructure budget totals **\$20,800,000** (\$8,300,000 Developer + \$12,500,000 City). Off-site improvements are critical to Phase I and must be completed on a timeline coordinated with the Phase I construction schedule. The Developer will work closely with the City\'s Community Development Department and Public Works Department to develop a construction sequencing plan that minimizes disruption and ensures that utility connections and roadway improvements are available when needed for Phase I occupancy.

**[Section 9: Compliance and Regulatory Considerations]{.underline}**

**[9.1 Prevailing Wage]{.underline}**

The Developer acknowledges that construction within the Lakemont Central TIF District is subject to applicable provisions of the **Illinois Prevailing Wage Act** (820 ILCS 130/). The Developer commits to compliance with prevailing wage requirements for all construction activities associated with the Lakemont Station Commons project. The Developer will require all contractors and subcontractors performing work on the project to comply with the prevailing wage rates established by the Illinois Department of Labor for Lake County, and will include prevailing wage compliance provisions in all construction contracts. The Developer will maintain certified payroll records and submit them to the City on a periodic basis as required by law and the development agreement.

**[9.2 Zoning and Entitlements]{.underline}**

As described in Section 3.3, the project site is currently zoned M-2 (General Manufacturing), which is incompatible with the proposed mixed-use program. The Developer will cooperate with the City Community Development Department to pursue rezoning to **PD-TOD (Planned Development--Transit Oriented District)**. The PD-TOD classification is expected to accommodate the proposed density, building heights, use mix, and design standards. Rezoning must be completed and approved by the City Council prior to execution of the development agreement. The Developer anticipates filing the rezoning application promptly upon selection as the preferred developer.

**[9.3 Environmental Compliance]{.underline}**

As detailed in Section 3.2, the Developer will enroll Parcel 12-24-300-016 in the **IEPA Voluntary Site Remediation Program (VSRP)** and prepare a Remedial Action Plan (RAP) for IEPA review and approval. A **No Further Remediation (NFR) letter** must be obtained from IEPA prior to residential development on Parcel 016. The Developer anticipates that IEPA approval of the remediation plan will be a condition precedent to land conveyance, consistent with the City Council\'s anticipated requirements. All environmental remediation activities will be conducted in full compliance with applicable IEPA regulations and the terms of the VSRP enrollment.

**[Section 10: Project Schedule Summary and Key Milestones]{.underline}**

The following table consolidates all key project milestones, presenting both the Developer\'s target accelerated schedule and the contractual maximum deadlines that would govern under the development agreement:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Milestone**                                         **Target Date**                     **Contractual Deadline**
  ----------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------- ----------------------------------------
  Rezoning to PD-TOD                                    Q2 2025                             Prior to DA execution

  Development Agreement Execution                       June 2025                           ---

  Land Conveyance                                       Q3 2025                             Following DA execution

  Environmental Remediation Commencement (Parcel 016)   Q3 2025                             Prior to Phase I vertical construction

  Phase I Groundbreaking                                Q1 2026                             No later than March 1, 2026

  Phase I Substantial Completion                        Q3 2028                             September 30, 2028 (30 months)

  Phase II Commencement                                 Q4 2028 (target)                    Within 12 months of Phase I SC

  Phase II Substantial Completion                       Q4 2030 (target)                    24 months from Phase II commencement

  Phase III Commencement                                Q1 2031 (target)                    Within 12 months of Phase II SC

  Phase III Substantial Completion                      Q3 2033 (target)                    30 months from Phase III commencement

  Phase IV Commencement                                 Q1 2031 (concurrent w/ Phase III)   Concurrent with Phase III

  Phase IV Substantial Completion                       Q1 2033 (target)                    24 months from Phase IV commencement

  TIF District Expiration                               ---                                 December 31, 2042
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Under the contractual maximum timeline scenario (assuming each phase utilizes the full permitted commencement window and construction duration), Phase II Substantial Completion would extend to approximately September 2031, Phase III Substantial Completion to approximately March 2035, and Phase IV Substantial Completion to approximately September 2034. The Developer\'s target accelerated schedule is approximately two years ahead of these maximum dates.

The milestone table illustrates that even under the maximum contractual timeline, all phases reach Substantial Completion well in advance of the TIF District\'s December 31, 2042 expiration date, allowing multiple years of full-buildout TIF increment collection. The Developer is committed to maintaining the accelerated schedule as its primary business objective, while the contractual maximums provide the flexibility necessary to navigate a decade-long development program.

**[Section 11: Conclusion and Request for Selection]{.underline}**

Granite City Development LLC is committed to delivering **Lakemont Station Commons** as a transformative transit-oriented development that will catalyze economic growth, provide critically needed affordable housing, create construction and permanent employment opportunities, remediate a brownfield site, and deliver lasting public amenities to the Lakemont community.

The Developer\'s experienced leadership team, under the direction of Marcus J. Pellegrini, brings nearly three decades of successful Chicagoland development experience spanning mixed-use, transit-oriented, and affordable housing projects. The project is supported by a robust capital structure, including **\$85 million in committed equity** from the Pellegrini Family Trust and Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP, a **\$210 million preliminary construction loan commitment** from First Prairie National Bank, and anticipated public incentives that reflect the shared public-private investment in the project\'s success.

The Lakemont Station Commons program --- 530 residential units, 280,000 square feet of Class A office, 63,000 square feet of retail, a 150-room hotel, a 2.5-acre public plaza, a 28,000 square-foot community center, and 1,430 structured parking spaces --- represents a comprehensive vision for the Lakemont Central Metra station area that exceeds the City\'s RFP requirements in scale, quality, public benefit, and sustainability commitment.

The Developer looks forward to working with **Mayor Ostrowski**, **City Manager Fong**, **Director Vasquez**, and the members of the **Lakemont City Council** to finalize the development agreement and bring this project to fruition. Lakemont Station Commons will be a source of community pride and a model for transit-oriented development in the Chicago metropolitan region.

Granite City Development LLC respectfully requests selection as the **preferred developer** for the Lakemont Central Metra station site.

Respectfully submitted,

GRANITE CITY DEVELOPMENT LLC

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: Marcus J. Pellegrini

Title: Manager and Chief Executive Officer

Date: December 16, 2024

**[Appendix A: Developer Entity Documentation]{.underline}**

The following entity and financial documentation is referenced in this proposal and is provided separately under **Tab A** of this submission:

> 1\. **Certificate of Organization and Certificate of Good Standing** --- Granite City Development LLC, organized under the laws of the State of Illinois on March 14, 2019, as evidenced by the Certificate of Organization filed with the Illinois Secretary of State. A current Certificate of Good Standing is included.
>
> 2\. **Operating Agreement Summary** --- Summary excerpts from the Operating Agreement of Granite City Development LLC, identifying the three members and their respective ownership interests: Pellegrini Family Trust (60%), Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP (25%), and TransitWorks Equity Group LLC (15%).
>
> 3\. **Evidence of Bonding Capacity** --- Letter from Ridgepoint Surety Corporation confirming bonding capacity of up to \$75,000,000 for the Lakemont Station Commons project.
>
> 4\. **Audited Financial Statements** --- Reference to the most recent audited financial statements of Granite City Development LLC, prepared by Clearmont Accounting Group LLP, reflecting an entity net worth of approximately \$185,000,000. Complete audited financials are available for confidential review upon request.

**[Appendix B: Preliminary Site Plan and Phasing Diagram]{.underline}**

The preliminary site plan for Lakemont Station Commons organizes the 12.7-acre site into four development zones corresponding to the four project phases:

> **Phase I** is located on the eastern portion of the site, occupying Parcel 12-24-300-015 and the eastern portions of Parcel 12-24-300-016. This location places Residential Tower A and the retail podium in the closest proximity to the Lakemont Central Metra station entrance, maximizing transit connectivity for the project\'s first residents and retail tenants.
>
> **Phase II** occupies the northern portion of the site, where the Class A office building, additional retail, and structured parking will front on Lakemont Station Boulevard. The northern positioning provides prominent commercial visibility and convenient access from the widened boulevard.
>
> **Phase III** is located on the western portion of the site, encompassing Parcel 12-24-300-017 and the remaining western portions of Parcel 016. Residential Tower B and the select-service hotel will benefit from views to the west and south, with direct pedestrian access to the central public plaza.
>
> **Phase IV** is centrally located, serving as the connective tissue between the three surrounding development phases. The 2.5-acre public plaza, 28,000 square-foot community center, and 600-space public parking structure occupy the heart of the site, creating a civic center accessible from all directions and serving as the focal point for the entire Lakemont Station Commons community.

Full architectural site plans and phasing diagrams are provided separately under **Tab B** of this submission.

**[Appendix C: Letters of Intent and Financial Commitments]{.underline}**

The following commitment letters and financial documentation are provided separately under **Tab C** of this submission:

> 1\. **Letter of Intent --- Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP.** Equity commitment of \$51,000,000, signed by Richard \"Rick\" Dominguez, Managing Partner, Lakeshore Capital Management LLC (General Partner of Lakeshore Capital Partners Fund III LP). Dated November 22, 2024.
>
> 2\. **Letter of Commitment --- Pellegrini Family Trust.** Equity commitment of \$34,000,000, signed by Marcus J. Pellegrini, Trustee. Dated November 18, 2024.
>
> 3\. **Preliminary Construction Loan Term Sheet --- First Prairie National Bank.** Construction loan facility of \$210,000,000, subject to customary conditions including execution of development agreement, satisfactory appraisal, and construction loan due diligence. Relationship Manager: Sandra L. Chen, Senior Vice President. Dated December 2, 2024.
>
> 4\. **Bonding Letter --- Ridgepoint Surety Corporation.** Confirmation of bonding capacity of up to \$75,000,000 for performance and payment bonds in connection with the Lakemont Station Commons project. Dated November 25, 2024.

END OF PROPOSAL --- PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL Granite City Development LLC --- RFP No. 2024-CD-07 Lakemont Station Commons
